races.race_name: Queen Mary Stakes (Group 2) (Fillies)

runners.name: Victorious
runners.spotlight: Both wins at Naas over 6f on good ground, the latest a Group 3 with a tradition of delivering Royal Ascot winners; that was no more than workmanlike and dropping back a furlong is a nagging concern when she's bred to stay much further; however, 2yos from this yard are notorious for improving with experience and their first Queen Mary winner last year was beaten in her first two races; commands respect for a whole host of reasons.
runners.comment: Both wins at Naas over 6f on good ground, the latest a Group 3; no doubt more to come

runners.name: Wild Blossom
runners.spotlight: Recruited at the breeze-ups for 300,000gns; faced only three rivals at Carlisle (good; 15-8) but hammered Crownbreaker by 10l and is held in high regard by her trainer who has won two of the last four Queen Marys with once-raced fillies; James Doyle prefers her over fellow debut winner Alta Regina; possible negatives are that the time of her debut win didn't blow your socks off and her sire Mehmas has a poor record at this meeting.
runners.comment: The time wasn't great but she breezed home by 10l on her debut at Carlisle

runners.name: Velozee
runners.spotlight: Made it 2-2 in a small-field Listed race at the Curragh (5f, good) before appearing to be stretched by the extra furlong when behind Victorious at Naas (Group 3); back over the minimum trip and, with her trainer always respected on his British forays, she could have some role to play.
runners.comment: 2-2 over 5f and appeared stretched by the extra furlong when behind Victorious since
runners.owner: Gary Matthews, Steve Weston & Lucky Hat

runners.name: Pershaada
runners.spotlight: Beaten favourite in AW contests won by Lover Girl and Love A Giggle; improved to win cosily in a respected time at Goodwood (good) but runner-up Kentucky Rain looked unlucky, and this filly may lack the same star potential as some in here.
runners.comment: Took three goes to win and probably lacks the same star potential as some in here

runners.name: Love A Giggle
runners.spotlight: First foal of a 5f 2yo winner; one of two runners for Karl Burke who knows what's required for this; followed up her debut success on the AW with a last-gasp win from Armor Supreme off a strong pace in the Listed Marygate at York (good; held up); looks sure to face a similar scenario here, and having the benefit of two races might give her an edge over some.
runners.comment: Made it 2-2 in securing Listed honours at York, coming from behind off a strong pace

runners.name: Ruiva
runners.spotlight: Clockwatchers were drooling after she won stylishly by 7l in blinkers on dirt (officially sloppy) over 4.5f at Keeneland in April and she's reportedly worked as impressively on grass; possesses the trademark early speed associated with Wesley Ward's Royal Ascot runners and, as a largely unknown quantity, she has to be of major interest; trainer has won this four times.
runners.comment: Won stylishly by 7l on the dirt at Keeneland and has reportedly worked as well on grass

runners.name: Lover Girl
runners.spotlight: Relinquished her unbeaten record behind Love A Giggle in the Marygate at York (good) but racing close to a fierce pace appeared to soften her up in the finish and she might be better than that.
runners.comment: Chased a strong pace when third at York, so she might be better than that

runners.name: Shining Moment
runners.spotlight: Her sire is Golden Pal who was second in the Norfolk at this meeting; this filly has 6.5l to find with More Champagne on their Keeneland clash but it was no-nonsense stuff when off the mark next time at Churchill Downs (5f, firm); the fact Wesley Ward has brought her over heightens interest, as does the booking of Oisin Murphy, though her trainer does appear sweeter on the chances of Ruiva; wore blinkers for both her races.
runners.comment: Left debut form behind to win easily at Churchill Downs; her sire nearly won the Norfolk
runners.owner: Qatar Racing, Breeze Easy & Riley J Ward

runners.name: Envision
runners.spotlight: Didn't look a Queen Mary candidate when only fourth at Thirsk and it was a winnable race that she took in first-time blinkers at Windsor (good to firm) since; that form is none too convincing heading into an assignment of this magnitude.
runners.comment: Won in new blinkers at Windsor (good to firm) but that form isn't overly convincing

runners.name: Fast Track
runners.spotlight: Both runs on good; has ground to make up on Pershaada and Kentucky Rain whom she trailed at Goodwood on debut and she probably has her work cut out to reverse Nottingham placings with Senorita Bonita, having dictated matters up front that day.
runners.comment: Pacey filly with confirmed useful ability but she does look held by some of these

runners.name: More Champagne
runners.spotlight: US challenger; accepted a lead before quickening away to a 6.5l defeat of subsequent winner Shining Moment at Keeneland (5.5f, firm) in April and there was an even wider margin back to the third; it's hard not to be impressed by that performance and her trainer seems confident of a bold show; makes the shortlist.
runners.comment: Quickened away to a 6.5l defeat of subsequent winner Shining Moment at Keeneland

runners.name: Senorita Bonita
runners.spotlight: Cost a whopping 900,000gns at the breeze-ups; by Royal Ascot winner Starspangledbanner; all the rage in the betting at Nottingham (good; 10-11 favourite) and got the job done, though left it late to produce a telling late kick from well off the pace to deny Fast Track; open to copious amounts of improvement and she's bred to be suited by this quicker surface; stable has had success with 2yos at this meeting.
runners.comment: Left it late to justify favouritism at Nottingham but she produced a telling late kick

runners.name: Alta Regina
runners.spotlight: 240,000gns breeze-up 2yo who's bred to be sharp, out of a 2yo 5f winner and a half-sister to two 2yo winners; she duly travelled strongly throughout at Lingfield (good to firm; 5-4 favourite) at the end of last month and bounded clear in a smart time to collect by a shade over 4l; rejected by James Doyle who favours Wild Blossom but it was probably a close call.
runners.comment: Won as she liked when favourite at Lingfield and the time was good

runners.name: Armor Supreme
runners.spotlight: Both runs on good ground; looks a bargain at 4,000euros, after making a winning debut at Goodwood and doing well to go down by only a head to Love A Giggle in York's Listed Marygate Stakes having raced closer to the strong pace than the winner; while it wouldn't be a surprise should she reverse those placings, there are lots of others to worry about.
runners.comment: Could reverse York placings with Love A Giggle but there are lots of others to worry about
runners.owner: Team Valor International, D Dias,M Eves

runners.name: Crystal Queen
runners.spotlight: Both runs on good to firm; second to a smart colt on Ayr debut and showed the benefit of that experience when nicely on top in Beverley's Hilary Needler; further progress is a distinct possibility.
runners.comment: Nicely on top to win Beverley's Hilary Needler; further progress is a distinct possibility

runners.name: Drazinda
runners.spotlight: French raider who prevailed by a length on very soft ground at Chantilly a month ago, but faces very different conditions here and the horse she beat has since come up short at Listed level; on the plus side, her hugely respected trainer wouldn't be bringing her over for the fun of it.
runners.comment: French raider who prevailed by a length on very soft ground at Chantilly a month ago

runners.name: Big Negotiator
runners.spotlight: Favourite for all her races and off the mark with a narrow verdict in a York novice last Friday (5f, good), beating a newcomer with the pair clear; that's not the preparation for a typical Queen Mary winner and it's unlikely that she'll be good enough anyway; cheekpieces enlisted.
runners.comment: Off the mark at the third attempt at York only last Friday; unlikely to be up to this

runners.name: Bint Archange
runners.spotlight: Third to Magic Effort at Newmarket before winning by 4l over C&D (good) in early May; having course experience will come in handy but she has bundles to find on the figures, probably too much.
runners.comment: Won easily over C&D in May but has plenty to find on the figures, probably too much

runners.name: Celtic Dispute
runners.spotlight: Blinkered for both runs; beaten first time out (odds-on favourite) but took the rise in class in her stride when flooring one of Wesley Ward's (debut winner) over 5f on firm ground in the Royal Palm at Gulfstream in early May, with the pair dominant; her trainer, who had the third in this 12 months ago (who also won the Royal Palm, as did compatriot Crimson Advocate who took this three years ago), describes her as a "big, strong filly"; intriguing on a few counts.
runners.comment: Impressed in final run in the USA and the trainer had the third in this 12 months ago

runners.name: Princesse d'Orange
runners.spotlight: Easily made all on Beverley debut (good to soft) and went off too hard for her own good when fading into midfield in the Marygate at York (3-1); ridden differently in a Listed race at Sandown (good) and looked threatening until flattening out late on; career best is needed and by some margin.
runners.comment: Third in a Listed race last time; career best is needed and by some margin

runners.name: Havana Lightning
runners.spotlight: Favourite when fifth behind Magic Effort on debut and left that form behind to make all for a comfortable win at Yarmouth (good); it's hard to believe a repeat of that will suffice at this level, for all that it was encouraging to see the second score readily next time.
runners.comment: Made all at Yarmouth; it's hard to believe a repeat of that will suffice at this level

runners.name: Magic Effort
runners.spotlight: Both runs on good ground; had some of these behind when winning at Newmarket's Craven meeting and perhaps it is best to turn a blind eye to Goodwood fourth in the novice won by Pershaada as she got restless in the stalls; she still rates an unlikely winner.
runners.comment: Newmarket winner who can be forgiven her defeat next time; still opposable at this level

runners.name: Crownbreaker
runners.spotlight: 550,000gns yearling whose siblings include July Cup winner Mill Stream; very likeable debut effort on good to firm at Newmarket, but couldn't hold a candle to Wild Blossom at Carlisle and it's rare for a maiden to win this.
runners.comment: Couldn't hold a candle to Wild Blossom at Carlisle and it's rare for a maiden to win this

runners.name: Miss Lizzy
runners.spotlight: Tongue tied for her last three races; AW winner who ran her best race when beaten only 2l in a French Listed race 17 days ago (good), having been held up well off the pace; has further races in her but this is unlikely to be one of them.
runners.comment: Beaten 2l in French Listed race last time; unlikely to be winning this

runners.name: Niewiadoma
runners.spotlight: Won well at Thirsk where she raced prominently and she was slow to break in the Marygate at York (good ground) after getting restless in the stalls; probably making up the numbers, however.
runners.comment: Debut winner who had excuses at York, but probably making up the numbers here

runners.name: Shimmering Sun
runners.spotlight: Only 3-1 at Salisbury (good) and she won by a length after leading a furlong out; the second was clear of the remainder but she's now 0-4; could be favourably boxed in 25 but may need more than just a good draw.
runners.comment: Won at Salisbury on her debut but the runner-up is now 0-4

runners.name: Kentucky Rain
runners.spotlight: 25-1 at Goodwood (good) but defied those odds with a hugely promising run behind the more experienced Pershaada, as this filly found a good bit of bother in running and might otherwise have beaten the winner who clocked a good time; with that know-how under her belt and granted a clear run at it this afternoon, she could be a lively one; bought by Amo Racing for £380,000 at the Goffs London Sale on Monday.
runners.comment: 25-1 at Goodwood but she might have beaten Pershaada with a trouble-free passage

races.race_name: Queen's Vase (Group 2)

runners.name: Del Maro
runners.spotlight: Acts on good to firm; sole win from seven races came at odds-on in a Yarmouth novice in April from newcomer Point Of Law; useful form most starts and he lost out by just a nose in four-runner Listed race at Goodwood (1m3f; sole start in cheekpieces) last time, registering his highest rating; also likely to stay this trip but other candidates seem to bring more potential.
runners.comment: Useful and likely to stay this trip but other candidates seem to bring more potential

runners.name: Limestone
runners.spotlight: Unraced on firmer than good; won the last three of his five races, making the running when asked serious questions for his first two wins; lost the lead at halfway on latest outing but rallied in grand style to emerge from fourth of five in that Listed race at Navan (1m5f, yielding) and firmly seize the initiative from Asakir late on; looks sure to stay; progressive and has a leading form chance after that latest start, while leaving the firm impression he has more in the locker.
runners.comment: Mastered Asakir in 1m5f Listed race last time; leading form chance and more in the locker

runners.name: Port Of Spain
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner who came up short in three Group events as 2yo; 15-2 in cheekpieces for his seasonal/handicap debut in London Gold Cup at Newbury (1m2f, good; unraced on firmer) last month, plugging on for a creditable fifth of ten; not far off the best of these on form, as he's kept on improving his rating in defeat, and powerful stable had five possible runners before the declaration stage; very smart half-sister Free Wind won at up to an extended 1m6f, too, but this one still doesn't seem the most striking candidate.
runners.comment: Not far off best form in this and yard is so strong, but not the most striking candidate
runners.owner: Westerberg & Mrs Magnier & M Tabor & D Smith

runners.name: Galiyan
runners.spotlight: Green fourth of five in novice at Newmarket (1m2f, good) in April on debut, but he kept finding for pressure in a maiden at Chester (1m4f, good) three weeks later and asserted in good style late on from the odds-on favourite; promising and, on Chester evidence, this longer trip will suit him well.
runners.comment: Promising and, on evidence of Chester 1m4f maiden win, this longer trip will suit him well

runners.name: Asakir
runners.spotlight: All three starts have been on softer than good; made most for a clearcut 1m2f maiden win at Leopardstown in April on second outing; that form has been boosted, including by him when second of five in Listed race at Navan (1m5f, yielding) but today's rival Limestone mastered him in the last half furlong; each-way candidate and cheekpieces go on.
runners.comment: Maiden winner; outgunned late on by Limestone in 1m5f Navan Listed race; cheekpieces now
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Mr Colonel
runners.spotlight: Has twice made the running, including when he had to battle for his short-head win at 11-10 in a novice at Redcar (1m2f, good to firm) in April; contested Group 3 events either side of that when outgunned by Del Maro among others at Newmarket (1m2f) in October but turning the tables on him in Chester Vase (1m4f, good) six weeks ago; those were very much supporting roles but he keeps galloping, so this trip may well suit; also tongue tied first time.
runners.comment: Very much supporting roles in two Group 3s but he keeps galloping, so 1m6f may well suit

runners.name: Wareeth
runners.spotlight: Stretched 14l clear on debut at Newcastle (1m, AW) in March and 6l clear at Salisbury (1m2f, good; made all) in another novice event five weeks ago; sixth of seven in Listed race at Newmarket (good; 4-1) in between is a stumbling block, but that was 1m1f and he looks far more like a stayer; others have markedly better form but he's not entirely ruled out.
runners.comment: 2-2 in novices; sixth of seven in 1m1f Listed race but he looks far more like a stayer

runners.name: Point Of Law
runners.spotlight: By Frankel out of useful 1m2f winner; gave odds-on Del Maro something to think about when half-length second in a Yarmouth novice (1m2f, good) in April; that was a bright start to career and he probably didn't have to show that form when going one better at 4-7 himself in a Newbury maiden (again 1m2f on good) five weeks later; still had something to learn last time and he has plenty more to offer but today's rivals and extra 4f ask serious questions.
runners.comment: Gave odds-on Del Maro something to think about on debut, before his own 1m2f odds-on win
runners.owner: HM The King & HM The Queen

runners.name: Magnetude
runners.spotlight: Sent handicapping this term and had 1m4f wins at Wolverhampton (AW) and Chester (good) on two of those three starts; gamely made all with first-time cheekpieces in the latter but that was off a mark of just 76 and, even though the form has been boosted, this race demands something of a much higher order.
runners.comment: Chester 1m4f handicap winner last time; this race demands something of a much higher order

runners.name: Ravenspire
runners.spotlight: Sea The Stars half-brother to Imperial Sovereign (RPR 99) who was sixth in this two years ago; easily won at Southwell (1m4f, AW) in March and, having been the first ridden along, eventually worked his way to the front at Haydock (good to firm) in April in another 1m4f novice; 2-2 and surely a stayer in the making but whether he's good enough for this is in major doubt.
runners.comment: 2-2 in 1m4f novices and surely a stayer in the making but he has lots to find on form
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Ranga Tang
runners.spotlight: Fifth attempt when short-head winner at 8-13 in a maiden at Salisbury (1m4f, good to firm) in early May; may well stay and had pretty useful form twice before that latest start but nothing to suggest a prominent finish in this company; his owners (who also run Magnetude) had a big-priced outsider in the last two runnings of this race and they finished seventh and sixth.
runners.comment: 1m4f maiden winner latest who may well stay but nothing suggests a prominent finish today

races.race_name: Duke Of Cambridge Stakes (Group 2) (Fillies & Mares)

runners.name: Blue Bolt
runners.spotlight: Unraced at two; progressive filly who won Sandown Listed race (good to firm) last July and rounded off 2025 with fine Group 1 second at Newmarket (good; Cathedral fifth) in October; asserted late to beat Kon Tiki going away at Goodwood (also 1m, good; Shes Perfect third, Arisaig fourth) on her comeback last month, indicating she's set for another good campaign; leading contender.
runners.comment: Progressive filly whose comeback win indicated she's set for another good term; big player

runners.name: Friendly Soul
runners.spotlight: 4-5 in 2024, when her wins included a Group 3 over C&D (good to firm) in July and the Group 1 Prix de l'Opera at Longchamp (1m2f, very soft) in October; came back from a 594-day absence only to endure a miserable experience at Haydock last month, when putting her foot in a hole and swiftly pulled up; thankfully connections reported her to be fine after that and she commands the utmost respect on this second attempt at a relaunch.
runners.comment: 4-5 in 2024, including C&D G3 and French G1; recent comeback a write-off; utmost respect

runners.name: Snellen
runners.spotlight: Two starts at Royal Ascot have yielded a win in the 2023 Chesham (7f) and cracking second of 23 in the Kensington Palace (1m, also good to firm) last year; won four-runner Leopardstown Group 3 (1m1f, good) the following month and there was plenty of encouragement when third in 7f Group 3 back there on last month's return; no surprise if she raises her game once more back at her favourite fixture.
runners.comment: Two starts at Royal Ascot yielded a win and cracking 2nd; encouraging return; can go well

runners.name: Carolina Jetstream
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner on AW but it was a personal best tackling turf for the fifth time when 66-1 second of 12 in Curragh Group 2 (1m, good; Jancis fifth, Francophone behind) 24 days ago; had a fairly soft lead that day, so it will be interesting to see if she can back it up under Ryan Moore.
runners.comment: Personal best when 2nd in 1m G2 latest; interesting if she backs it up under Ryan Moore
runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: Cathedral
runners.spotlight: One Lingfield maiden win doesn't do this talented filly justice, with her fourth in the Group 1 Coronation over C&D at last year's Royal Ascot just one of many cracking efforts; built on this season's reappearance when chasing home Jancis in Newmarket Group 2 (1m1f, good to firm) last month and she certainly has the ability to have a say again.
runners.comment: One maiden win doesn't do her justice and she certainly has the ability to have a say

runners.name: Godspeed
runners.spotlight: French filly who did really well in her first season's racing last year, winning Chantilly Group 2 (good to soft) in June and coming home fourth in the Group 1 Prix Rothschild (again 1m, good) in August; reappeared when only just failing to snatch 7f Listed event at Longchamp (good to soft) in April after which Wathnan made an offer that was evidently hard to refuse; interesting.
runners.comment: G2 winner; only just failed in 7f Listed on return; set to kick on this year; interesting

runners.name: Catalina Delcarpio
runners.spotlight: Didn't make the track at two; quickly established herself as a smart filly last year and she went off favourite when third in the Ribblesdale (1m4f, good to firm) at this meeting; campaigned at 1m this term and she made Listed-race breakthrough at Leopardstown (good) against males last month; that was just her fifth start and it's likely there is better to come.
runners.comment: Third in 1m4f G2 here last year; won 1m Listed last time; likely there is better to come

runners.name: Noche Clasica
runners.spotlight: She is on her third trainer but has won half of her eight starts, most recently when picking up a Musselburgh handicap (1m1f, good) on her comeback in April; showed she belongs at Listed level when beaten 2l at Haydock (10.5f, good to soft) last time but, while she continues to make strides, this is a different test altogether.
runners.comment: Belongs at Listed level but, while she continues to make strides, this is a different test

runners.name: Shes Perfect
runners.spotlight: All runs last year were in France; suffered heartbreaking demotion for connections when placed second in the French 1,000 Guineas at Longchamp (1m, good) in May and failed to stay 10.5f in the Diane the following month; not seen again until encouraging third behind Blue Bolt and Kon Tiki at Goodwood last month but suffered a heavy defeat at Epsom just 11 days ago; ground a plausible excuse that day and new cheekpieces may help her bounce back.
runners.comment: "Won" French 1,000 Gns last year; solid return and latest can be forgiven; cheekpieces now

runners.name: Jancis
runners.spotlight: Tends to go well fresh and it was a clear career best when reappearing to win 1m1f Group 2 at Newmarket (good to firm; Cathedral second, Francophone fourth, Falakeyah sixth) in May; didn't quite match that form when fifth in similar company at the Curragh three weeks later, albeit with that race not panning out ideally; 3lb penalty will likely leave her vulnerable again, though.
runners.comment: Career best to win 1m1f G2 on her return but 3lb penalty makes life tricky in this company

runners.name: Falakeyah
runners.spotlight: All turf starts on good or firmer; looked on the path to stardom when making virtually all to win readily in last year's Listed Pretty Polly at Newmarket (1m2f) in May but she disappointed when just 5-2 in the Group 1 Coronation over C&D at this meeting the following month, beaten nearly 14l; similar story at the same price when only sixth to Jancis on return in May but she's in good hands and those Pretty Polly memories still linger.
runners.comment: Looked a star on last year's return; disappointing subsequently but she's in good hands

runners.name: Kon Tiki
runners.spotlight: Lost her unbeaten record when only seventh of 11 in last year's Group 1 Coronation over C&D at the Royal meeting but otherwise her light career has been one of progress; reeled in late by Blue Bolt in a Goodwood Listed race (1m, good) last month and it's likely she's capable of better again; considered.
runners.comment: Below best over C&D at last year's Royal meeting but otherwise progressive; considered

runners.name: Arisaig
runners.spotlight: Smart hold-up mare who sometimes hits traffic; matched previous best when second in a Newmarket handicap on her April return and latest fourth of six behind Blue Bolt in Goodwood Listed event (also 1m, good) was a solid effort; stall 1 doesn't make life easy but it wouldn't be a huge shock to see her appear on the scene late if able to pick her way through.
runners.comment: Smart hold-up mare who can hit traffic; stall 1 not ideal, but has the ability to run well

runners.name: Dash Of Azure
runners.spotlight: The majority of her starts have been at 7f but she kept on to finish fifth of 25 in the Sandringham over the straight mile here (good to firm) at last year's Royal Ascot; peak effort came on her return to this track when just failing to win 7f Listed event (soft; unlucky) in October but she'll need to put an underwhelming comeback behind her to make an impact in this first Group 2.
runners.comment: Peak effort when just failing in 7f Listed race here; very underwhelming return, though

runners.name: Francophone
runners.spotlight: Dual Listed winner, including over 1m at Newmarket (good to firm; Arisaig third) in September; finished 10l adrift of Jancis on return in May but latest tenth of 12 in Curragh Group 2 (1m, good) is better than it reads, having been tapped for toe then getting no room when launching a rally; still likely to find a few too good.
runners.comment: Latest tenth in Curragh Group 2 (1m) can be upgraded; still likely to find a few too good

races.race_name: Prince Of Wales's Stakes (Group 1)

runners.name: Ombudsman
runners.spotlight: Won this by 2l last year (good to firm) and he's added two further Group 1 wins since; confirmed his wellbeing when defying a penalty in the Group 3 Brigadier Gerard at Sandown (1m2f, good) three weeks ago following a two-month break; this is a tougher line-up than 12 months ago and he was runner-up to Daryz's stablemate Calandagan in the Champion Stakes over C&D (good) last October, so he's not bombproof, but he has extremely strong credentials.
runners.comment: Won this by 2l last year; he's not bombproof but has extremely strong credentials

runners.name: Minnie Hauk
runners.spotlight: 1m4f Group 1 wins last summer in the Oaks, Irish Oaks and Yorkshire Oaks (good to soft-good to firm) before taking on males in the Arc at Longchamp (1m4f, very soft) and finishing a clear second to Daryz, beaten just a head; reappeared with a Group 2 win at the Curragh (1m2f, good) and, while she has something to prove after a disappointing run there in the Tattersalls Gold Cup (10.5f, good), she may well be in the mix if she's back to her best.
runners.comment: Beaten a head by Daryz in the Arc; disappointing last time but in the mix if back on song
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Daryz
runners.spotlight: He made it five wins from his first seven starts when winning the Arc at Longchamp (1m4f, very soft) last October, beating Minnie Hauk by a head, and he's confirmed that brilliance this season with comfortable Group 1 wins at Longchamp in the Prix Ganay (10.5f, good) and the Prix Aga Khan IV (1m1f, soft); this race will take a lot more winning than his last two contests and it's possible that 1m4f is his optimum trip, but he's a leading player nevertheless.
runners.comment: Arc winner; it's possible 1m4f is his optimum trip but he's a leading player nevertheless

runners.name: Almaqam
runners.spotlight: Last season concluded with fine third in the Champion Stakes over C&D (good; about 2l behind runner-up Ombudsman) and this 5yo looked better than ever when winning the Group 1 Tattersalls Gold Cup at the Curragh (10.5f, good) on his reappearance, with subsequent Coronation Cup winner Bay City Roller 2l back in second and a disappointing Minnie Hauk in fifth; today's company is even hotter but he might not be far away if the fast ground isn't an issue.
runners.comment: 5yo who was better than ever when winning the Tattersalls Gold Cup; might not be far away

runners.name: See The Fire
runners.spotlight: Secured back-to-back Group 2 Middleton wins at York (10.5f, good to firm/good) last month; went very close to making Group 1 breakthrough in the Prix de l'Opera at Longchamp (1m2f, very soft) last October but beaten just over 4l by Ombudsman when third in this last year (good to firm) and it's likely that her limitations will again be exposed today.
runners.comment: Held in third behind Ombudsman in this last year; limitations likely to be exposed again

runners.name: Devil's Advocate
runners.spotlight: He was the pacemaker for stablemate Ombudsman in the Champion Stakes over C&D last October, when seventh of 11 at 66-1, and that's set to be his role again this afternoon.
runners.comment: Pacemaker for Ombudsman, as was the case when seventh of 11 in the Champion Stakes

runners.name: Mississippi River
runners.spotlight: It was his first run above Listed class when making the running in the Group 1 Lockinge at Newbury (1m, good) last month, finishing last of ten; once more set for pacemaking duties, to assist stablemate Minnie Hauk.
runners.comment: Last of ten when a pacemaker in the Lockinge; set for same role today, for Minnie Hauk
runners.owner: D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & M Tabor & Peter M Brant

runners.name: Dancing Gemini
runners.spotlight: Kept on for fourth in first-time cheekpieces (retained today) in the Lockinge at Newbury (1m, good) last month, having been beaten just a neck in the same Group 1 in 2025; on that latest evidence this step back up in trip is worth a go, but others arrive with stronger claims.
runners.comment: Kept on for 4th in the Lockinge; return to 1m2f worth a go but others have stronger claims

races.race_name: Royal Hunt Cup (Heritage Handicap)

runners.name: Indalo
runners.spotlight: Unraced on softer than good; record is 4-11 (AW/turf); good second in the 2025 Cambridgeshire (would likely have won but for traffic issues) and Newbury event (collared by Classic) in last two appearances; progressive, represents last year's winning yard and has a big prize in him; respected.
runners.comment: Unlucky in the 2025 Cambridgeshire; ran well on reappearance; progressive and respected

runners.name: Blue Rc
runners.spotlight: Below par in the Newbury contest won by Classic last time but that was out of character for him; otherwise a solid and steadily progressive colt whose form this spring includes good efforts in AW finals event and the Thirsk Hunt Cup; whether he can defy current mark is another matter.
runners.comment: Uncharacteristically below par last time; otherwise a solid and steadily progressive colt

runners.name: La Botte
runners.spotlight: Has failed to build on an eye-catching reappearance effort but seemed to be outstayed over 1m2f most recently; unlucky second in the C&D Britannia at this meeting last year (good to firm; might well have won but for traffic issues) and looks very interesting back here, bidding for compensation.
runners.comment: Unlucky second in the 2025 Britannia; very interesting back here, bidding for compensation
runners.owner: Halo Racing N Greenhalgh J Degenhardt

runners.name: Rogue Diplomat
runners.spotlight: Failed to fire in French Listed race last time; broadly progressive in handicaps and went close in the Lincoln when bidding for a five-timer (ran creditably in Spring Cup next time); however, chance partly depends on the weather, as he's ideally suited by ground softer than good.
runners.comment: Has broadly progressive handicap form; ideally suited by ground softer than good

runners.name: Scoville
runners.spotlight: Commenced his career with a novice double, looking a smart prospect; disappointing in his three races (Group/conditions) since, gelded prior to latest, but is on an attractive mark assuming another physical tweak (wind surgery) prompts a turnaround and fulfilment of the early promise.
runners.comment: On an attractive mark assuming wind surgery prompts a fulfilment of his early promise
runners.owner: Sir M Arbib,B Arbib,C Budgett & A Kheir

runners.name: Linwood
runners.spotlight: Ended last season with an easy success (good to soft; subsequently gelded) and followed up in the Spring Cup at Newbury (good) in sole start this term, taking record over 1m to 3-5 and overall strike-rate to 4-9; may be capable of further progress at this distance; enters calculations.
runners.comment: Spring Cup win took his record over 1m to 3-5; may show further progress; major player

runners.name: Shout
runners.spotlight: Versatile as regards ground; perhaps didn't take to visor in the Britannia at this meeting 12 months ago; creditable fourth in major handicap last October when bidding for a C&D double; possibilities back in this scenario, especially as latest effort (third at York) represented a return to form.
runners.comment: Ran creditably last October when bidding for C&D double; back to form at York latest start

runners.name: Classic
runners.spotlight: Has less hidden up his sleeve than others, being a 6yo whose record is 4-24, but he arrives having been better than ever in useful handicaps at Newbury (1m, good) this term; finished second to Linwood on reappearance and weaved his way through to beat Indalo by a neck last time.
runners.comment: Better than ever in Newbury contests this term, last time beating Indalo by a neck

runners.name: Fifth Column
runners.spotlight: A regular in notable handicaps, bagging three (over 1m) during his progressive period last term and proving resurgent when close second to Erzindjan at Newmarket (1m1f) latest start; first home in the unfavoured group in the Britannia at this meeting 12 months ago; looks set to go well.
runners.comment: First home in the unfavoured group in last year's Britannia; resurgent effort latest start

runners.name: Mister Winston
runners.spotlight: Overcame trouble to win by 1l at Epsom (8.5f, good to soft) on Oaks day, taking record since being gelded to 2-3 and overall strike-rate to 4-12; returns to a hotter handicap but he's improving steadily and 1lb well in under a penalty; not dismissed.
runners.comment: Epsom win on Oaks day took his record since being gelded to 2-3; 1lb well in under penalty

runners.name: Excellent Believe
runners.spotlight: Successful at Haydock in sole handicap attempt then ran well behind a notable rival in Group 3 at same venue; no further progress in three runs since but he is proving reasonably consistent and returns to handicap level with Toby Moore taking off a useful 7lb; could go well.
runners.comment: Proving consistent; 1-1 at handicap level; Toby Moore takes off useful 7lb; could go well

runners.name: Erzindjan
runners.spotlight: Campaigned over 1m1f/1m2f for current yard; beat Fifth Column by half a length at Newmarket (good to firm) in sole run this season, ending a five-year losing spell and showing he retains plenty of ability aged eight; has form claims but doesn't seem to be crying out for this return to 1m.
runners.comment: Newmarket win shows he retains plenty of ability but this drop back to 1m looks a negative

runners.name: Cerulean Bay
runners.spotlight: Completed a Goodwood double during his 2025 campaign; good second in notable handicap at the York Dante festival (1m, good) most recently, going down by only a neck; he's paying the price for his consistency, however, with the assessor failing to relent.
runners.comment: Ran well in notable race at York last time but the assessor is failing to relent

runners.name: War Socks
runners.spotlight: Trained in Norway; consistent in handicaps this year, most recently winning a Swedish event after some good runs at Meydan; not disgraced at Royal Ascot (1m2f) or Glorious Goodwood (1m) last term but is on a much stiffer mark now.
runners.comment: Trained in Norway; not disgraced in two British attempts but is on a stiffer mark now

runners.name: Jagged Edge
runners.spotlight: Acts on soft ground; Irish 4yo who landed a major handicap at the Curragh during his time with Dermot Weld; won well at Naas (good) on stable/seasonal debut, taking form figures over 1m to 3111 and strike-rate to 3-6; interesting challenger with further improvement very plausible.
runners.comment: Irish 4yo who won well at Naas on stable/seasonal debut, taking record over 1m to 3111

runners.name: Witch Hunter
runners.spotlight: Defied a 5lb higher mark in the 7f handicap at this meeting three years ago and scored over 1m on AW in January; has lost his form, most recently finishing last in the Newbury event won by Classic; further Royal Ascot glory looks unlikely.
runners.comment: Won the 7f handicap at this meeting three years ago; has a doubt over current form

runners.name: Holloway Boy
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in C&D Listed contest last time, maintaining his consistency; Royal Ascot record includes a debut success in the 2022 Chesham and creditable fifth (off 7lb lower) in this race in 2024; Jack Nicholls takes off a useful 5lb; solid contender who again should give his running.
runners.comment: Consistent; creditable fifth in this race in 2024; Jack Nicholls takes off a useful 5lb
runners.owner: N White, S White, T White & E Burke

runners.name: Ebt's Guard
runners.spotlight: Dual winner at Ascot; hindered by trouble in the Spring Cup on reappearance and ran creditably kept to Newbury last time; far from disgraced when seventh in this contest last year but has a stiffer mark this time and his ceiling has seemingly been reached already.
runners.comment: Not disgraced when seventh in this contest last year but has a stiffer mark this time

runners.name: Urban Lion
runners.spotlight: Good fifth in this race last year but was in a progressive vein of form at the time; some inconsistency has crept in and he finished last (admittedly lost a shoe) in the Spring Cup at Newbury most recently, failing to back up the form of his narrow success in the Lincoln on reappearance.
runners.comment: Good fifth in this race last year and landed the 2026 Lincoln; dismal result latest start

runners.name: Godwinson
runners.spotlight: Got up in the final stride to win last year's Lincoln and followed up in useful AW handicap; not in the same form since and York defeat on stable/seasonal debut spoiled an otherwise good record when fresh; revival is far from guaranteed.
runners.comment: Won last year's Lincoln, for previous yard; needs to step up on York reappearance effort

runners.name: Ozat
runners.spotlight: Scored four times in France but has come up short in handicaps won by Linwood and Mister Winston since joining new stable, beaten about 7l both times; probably best watched until he's down the weights and his sights are lowered.
runners.comment: Ex-French 5yo who has come up short in two handicaps for new stable

runners.name: Henlein
runners.spotlight: Capable of very useful form in Australia, notably a close second in 1m2f Group 1 contest in 2024; lightly raced since and 40-1 when seventh of 12 in Haydock handicap (7f) on British debut last time; best watched in this deep field.
runners.comment: Ex-Australian 4yo who was a 40-1 shot when seventh of 12 at Haydock on British debut
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Chibitty
runners.spotlight: Broadly useful when trained in France but may be flattered by his biggest figure, having failed to back it up, latterly in two Meydan starts this year for new yard; something to prove off his mark in this first British attempt.
runners.comment: May be flattered by his biggest figure; something to prove off his mark

runners.name: Archivist
runners.spotlight: In good form at Meydan this year for new yard, most recently scoring readily in first-time hood to take his 1m record to 22111 (all wins on good/firmer); may have even more to offer over this trip in the retained headgear; sound claims.
runners.comment: Success in first-time hood at Meydan took his record over 1m to 22111; sound claims

runners.name: Thunder Run
runners.spotlight: Back to form with second at the York Dante festival (good) in the 1m2f event he'd won 12 months earlier; before racing over longer trips he was a three-time 1m winner and ran creditably in major handicap over C&D once; may remain competitive.
runners.comment: Back to form last time (1m2f); three-time 1m winner; ran creditably over C&D once

runners.name: One Smack Mac
runners.spotlight: Formerly trained by Ger Lyons; placed a few times over 6f/7f in Listed grade but posted duck eggs at Royal Ascot 2025 and in valuable Irish handicap in his last two appearances; sold for 80,000gns since; hood fitted; enough to prove on debut for new yard.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish 4yo who has enough to prove in this scenario on debut for new yard
runners.owner: Emma Banks, David Manasseh & Austin Whelan

runners.name: Skukuza
runners.spotlight: Good second (off 20lb lower) in the Britannia at this meeting two years ago but he was progressive at the time, whereas his form has dipped in last two outings (normally a consistent sort, including at Group level); 2-2 at the Curragh compared to only 1-16 elsewhere; wears first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Good second in Britannia here two years ago; form dipped in last two starts; headgear now

runners.name: No Retreat
runners.spotlight: Useful performer but has done much of his racing in the Middle East, winning three times; 0-5 in British events and suffered a heavy defeat (albeit with something possibly amiss) at Royal Ascot 12 months ago; something to prove off his mark.
runners.comment: Useful but has done much of his racing in the Middle East; 0-5 in British events

runners.name: Checkandchallenge
runners.spotlight: Very useful on his day and has plenty of Listed/Group form; however, needs to improve on his Newbury handicap efforts this season (beaten 4l both times) and remains badly out of the winning habit, with losing spell stretching back to 2023.
runners.comment: Very useful on his day but losing spell stretches back to 2023

runners.name: Swing Vote
runners.spotlight: Has been campaigned only on artificial surfaces, mostly in UAE, for current yard; generally out of form since a hat-trick that was over two years ago (missed the 2025 campaign); enough to prove in first turf attempt since his French days in 2023.
runners.comment: Campaigned on artificial surfaces for current yard; not seen on turf since 2023

races.race_name: Kensington Palace Stakes (Fillies' Handicap)

runners.name: Alobayyah
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced filly who ran an eye-catching trial for this when third of nine to Radiant Beauty and Song N Dance over C&D (good) on reappearance, doing well to finish so close to that prominently ridden pair having been held up in rear in a steadily run race; further improvement seems likely for top yard after only four starts; leading claims.
runners.comment: Eye-catching trial when third to Radiant Beauty over C&D, finishing well from too far back

runners.name: Seren Star
runners.spotlight: Largely progressed well in 2025 (a three-time winner at 1m-1m1f) but nowhere near her best when remote third in 1m1f Hamilton handicap on reappearance and the yard's main riders look elsewhere; tongue tied first time.
runners.comment: Three-time winner at 1m-1m1f last year but remote third on reappearance; tongue-tie added

runners.name: Cheshire Dancer
runners.spotlight: Third in this race on good to firm last year and left even that form behind when bagging a Group 3 over the round mile here under Billy Loughnane a month later; hasn't fired in two outings this spring but given a chance by the handicapper as a result and no shock to see a revival back at Ascot.
runners.comment: Hasn't fired this year but did well here in 2025, third in this before Group 3 win

runners.name: Stateira
runners.spotlight: Four wins on AW, including a valuable Newcastle handicap on Good Friday; proved herself on turf when third of 12 in 1m Group 2 at the Curragh (good) 24 days ago; subsequently purchased by Wathnan Racing; classy but she'll require a very smart handicap performance to defy such a lofty mark.
runners.comment: Four wins on AW and latest Curragh Group 2 third shows she's as good on turf; lofty mark

runners.name: Zgharta
runners.spotlight: A few of last year's efforts suggest this sort of mark is within reach; never threatened when favourite for the C&D handicap won by Radiant Beauty on reappearance but was caught further back than ideal the way that race went; the hood she's worn since her second start is off today; still not fully exposed.
runners.comment: Possible excuse when beaten favourite in C&D race won by Radiant Beauty; not fully exposed

runners.name: Radiant Beauty
runners.spotlight: Yet another success story for the James Owen yard, with her all-the-way defeat of Song N Dance and Alobayyah over C&D (good) last month her third win in just five starts for the stable; enjoyed the run of things from the front on that occasion but further progress isn't discounted.
runners.comment: Made it 3-5 for James Owen when beating Song N Dance and Alobayyah over C&D latest; up 6lb

runners.name: Sand Gazelle
runners.spotlight: Listed winner at York (1m2f, good) last July; even better form when runner-up in Group 3 at the Curragh (1m1f, good to yielding) the following month but she hasn't got near that level in three subsequent outings, including two this season; that has resulted in her mark easing but she needs to be back with a bang in first-time blinkers (replacing cheekpieces worn last time).
runners.comment: Listed win last year but has struggled in recent starts; blinkers need to give her a lift

runners.name: Oolong Poobong
runners.spotlight: Progressive handicap form at the start of last summer, winning convincingly at Haydock (7f, good) before excellent fourth of 25 in the Sandringham at this meeting (1m, good to firm); not seen again until a creditable fifth of 13 in 1m Thirsk handicap (good) last month and entitled to come on for that run; first-time tongue-tie worn at Thirsk is retained; high on the shortlist under Spencer.
runners.comment: 4th in 2025 Sandringham; off until 5th at Thirsk last month; entitled to strip fitter now

runners.name: Song N Dance
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 on the up with wins in 7f Leicester novice (good) and 1m Newcastle handicap (AW); has continued the good work this year, finishing runner-up in handicaps at Southwell and over C&D (good; split Radiant Beauty and Alobayyah); one to consider.
runners.comment: Steadily progressive, splitting Radiant Beauty and Alobayyah over C&D latest; claims

runners.name: Callianassa
runners.spotlight: All four wins have come over 7f/1m on the AW, the latest in February; now 2lb lower than for that success, but she took her turf record to 0-11 when only sixth of 11 at Chester (extended 1m2f) last time; this is more her trip but she's still hard to make a case for.
runners.comment: All four wins on AW and 0-11 on turf; others are preferred

runners.name: All Moonshine
runners.spotlight: Has improved with each of her four starts (all AW); completed her hat-trick when making all in a small-field fillies' handicap at Kempton (1m) in February; absent since (non-runner in a Kempton Listed race in March on a vet's certificate - lame); no reason on breeding why she won't prove as effective on turf; open to further progress.
runners.comment: Completed an AW hat-trick in February; off since;  unexposed now moving to turf

runners.name: Miss Nightfall
runners.spotlight: Has won only a novice in 14 starts but has some classy form, notably a close fifth in a Group 3 over 7f last September; favourite back in handicap company at Yarmouth (7f, good) last time but could manage only fifth of seven; her stable is no stranger to handicap success at this meeting but other runners arrive with more compelling claims.
runners.comment: 1-14 overall and only fifth when favourite for 7f Yarmouth handicap latest

runners.name: Rumba Numba
runners.spotlight: Made light of a ten-month absence in small-field Doncaster handicap (1m, good to firm) 12 days ago; this comes quite quick on the back of that but she's an unexposed sort from a leading stable that is in tremendous form.
runners.comment: Won at Doncaster recently on return from an absence and her yard is in excellent form

runners.name: Renesmee
runners.spotlight: Kept on well for second of 15 over an extended 1m4f at Chester (good) in May but below-par sixth over the same trip at Goodwood since; latest win came over 1m on AW in March, so the significant drop back in trip shouldn't be an issue but it's still hard to be upbeat about her prospects here from 4lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: Second over 1m4f last month but latest win came over 1m; below par latest; outsider

runners.name: Quamby
runners.spotlight: Maiden who returned from a break with a creditable fourth of nine to Radiant Beauty, Song N Dance and Alobayyah over C&D (good) last month but wasn't in the same form when filling the same position in a 1m1f Lingfield handicap since; 2lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: Maiden; good fourth to Radiant Beauty over C&D but not in the same form at Lingfield since

runners.name: Crystal Flyer
runners.spotlight: Consistent for Tom Ward in 2025; weakened quickly when tailed off in 1m4f Goodwood Listed race on return for new yard and, under a change of tactics, went off way too hard in 1m1f Hamilton handicap since; hooded first time; lots to prove.
runners.comment: Plenty to prove having been well held on both runs for this yard; hooded first time
runners.owner: Castle Racing Co Ltd & Mps Racing Ltd

runners.name: Lady Mariko
runners.spotlight: All three wins over shorter, the latest over 6f at Kempton (AW) in April; unable to reproduce that form in three 7f turf outings since; stays 1m but it's hard to be positive about her chance from 3lb out of the weights.
runners.comment: 6f win on AW in April but below par on turf since; stays 1m but it's hard to be positive

runners.name: American Gal
runners.spotlight: Steady progress culminated in close second to Cheshire Dancer in a Group 3 over the round mile here (good to firm) last July; not in the same form since (tailed off in Newmarket Group 2 latest) and it remains to be seen whether a return to Ascot sparks her back to life.
runners.comment: Neck second to Cheshire Dancer in Group 3 here last July but has struggled since

runners.name: Unassuming
runners.spotlight: C&D winner (good to firm) off 6lb lower last July but fourth was the best placing she achieved in five AW handicaps earlier this year; off 11 weeks; others make more obvious appeal.
runners.comment: C&D winner off 6lb lower last July but this year's AW efforts were not inspiring

runners.name: Gaga Girl
runners.spotlight: Second win of her career when seeing off 12 rivals in 1m1f Compiegne conditions race (good to soft) on reappearance and she backed that up with excellent second of ten in 1m Longchamp Listed race (very soft) five weeks ago; firmish ground would be an unknown for this French raider.
runners.comment: French raider who was been in career-best form this spring but firmish ground an unknown

runners.name: Perfect Part
runners.spotlight: No win in 16 starts since debut success last June but has run with credit on all four this year, including fourth in the Spring Mile at Doncaster (good to soft); second of 11 over 1m at Thirsk (1m, good) 17 days ago; each-way shout at likely decent odds.
runners.comment: 0-16 since debut success but has run with credit in four handicaps this year

runners.name: Rhapsody
runners.spotlight: Back on the up with a comfortable York handicap win (1m1f, good) off 9lb lower last September and ended her campaign with a good third of nine in 1m Listed race in Italy; has raced only on good or softer when on turf; lightly raced enough to think she may have more to offer this season.
runners.comment: Useful form last autumn and still fairly low mileage for a top stable

runners.name: Muddy Mooy
runners.spotlight: Both wins were on AW and she did well to finish third, in difficult circumstances, at Kempton (1m2f Listed, AW) on seasonal/stable debut; stiff task in Group 3 back on turf at Sandown since but she should still have done better than a remote last of five; fourth in this last year but off 7lb lower.
runners.comment: Well held on latest start and she's 7lb higher than when fourth in this last year

runners.name: Goldilocks Cen
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the fourth time of asking in France in 1m3f Dieppe maiden (heavy) last July; off since (sold out of Henri-Francois Devin's yard for 13,000euros in February); drops back significantly in trip for British and handicap debut; a hard one to assess and can really only be watched.
runners.comment: Off since 1m3f French win last July; a hard one to assess and can really only be watched

runners.name: Betty Clover
runners.spotlight: Won a Listed race at York (5f, good) as a 2yo but she's 0-14 since and was beaten 11l when fourth in Epsom Group 3 (8.5f, good to soft) 11 days ago.
runners.comment: Listed winner as a 2yo but 0-14 since; others are preferred

races.race_name: Windsor Castle Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Controlla
runners.spotlight: 160,000gns yearling; bred to be smart and she made a cracking debut when second of six in a Group 3 at Naas 31 days ago (6f, good), giving the odds-on favourite a fright in the process; that form puts her well clear on RPRs and she is open to improvement, so she has to be high on the list.
runners.comment: Cracking effort in Group 3 on debut (6f, good); clear on RPRs and improvement likely

runners.name: Sale Shark
runners.spotlight: 52,000euros yearling out of a mare who has produced a couple of smart sprinters; strong in the market and created a favourable impression in a 5f maiden (good to firm) at Hamilton three weeks ago when skipping clear of rivals with experience; 6f should suit him well and he is open to plenty of progress; one for the shortlist.
runners.comment: Created a good impression at Hamilton (5f); this is tougher but he looks a promising colt

runners.name: Sergei Diaghilev
runners.spotlight: 4-6 favourite for his Curragh debut (6f, good) 25 days ago and produced a professional, rather than spectacular, display in winning by half a length; his trainer suggested that there could be a lot more to come from the colt and the form has been franked by the second and third since; looks the part on paper and there is plenty to like.
runners.comment: Ready 6f win at the Curragh last month (form franked); open to improvement; obvious chance
runners.owner: Westerberg & Mrs Magnier & M Tabor & D Smith

runners.name: A Bear Affair
runners.spotlight: Kept on well to win the Brocklesby at Doncaster on debut in March (5f, good to soft); put in his place in a conditions event and the Listed National Stakes since; stepping up to 6f can help but it needs to.
runners.comment: Brocklesby winner; two defeats since would appear to highlight his vulnerability
runners.owner: A Bit On The Side & Partner

runners.name: Moonrise
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed a solo up front on her Kempton debut three weeks ago (6f, AW), coming home unchallenged; while promising, this is a big step up in class and others bring more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Easy front-running win on May's Kempton debut (6f, AW); promising but this is much tougher

runners.name: Green Sovereign
runners.spotlight: Midfield finish on his Kempton debut last month (6f, AW); outclassed his rivals in a 6f maiden at Pontefract (good to soft) 26 days ago, coming home 6l to the good despite drifting right across the track in the closing stages; good prospect but this is a big step up in class and he has forecast faster ground to contend with too.
runners.comment: Wide-margin win, despite hanging right, at Pontefract; up in class on faster ground today

runners.name: Celeron
runners.spotlight: Refused to enter the stalls on his intended debut at Navan last month; showed himself to be a useful juvenile when comfortably accounting for his 12 rivals at the Curragh a fortnight ago (6f, good to yielding), travelling strongly and finding plenty for pressure; has more to offer but is no banker to confirm those placings with runner-up One Number.
runners.comment: Travelled well and found plenty for his win at the Curragh on recent debut (6f); promising

runners.name: Charted Course
runners.spotlight: 450,000gns breeze-up purchase; 2-1 favourite for debut at Haydock (6f, soft) four weeks ago and ran creditably for second, paying the price for chasing an overly strong pace in the conditions; his sire's progeny have a stronger record on fast ground and he could take a step forward today but it is certainly needed.
runners.comment: Beaten favourite but ran with credit in Haydock novice (6f, soft); faster ground a plus

runners.name: Dorigo
runners.spotlight: Showed a fair level of ability when second of seven in a 6f AW maiden at Newcastle four weeks ago but he needs a marked step forward to feature in this field.
runners.comment: Promise when second in a 6f AW maiden at Newcastle four weeks ago; this is much tougher

runners.name: Ischgl
runners.spotlight: In the frame in three maidens (6f-6.3f, good and soft) in Ireland; can win races but he looks to be flying too high today.
runners.comment: Clear promise in three Irish starts but unlikely to be shedding maiden tag in this field

runners.name: Wild Terrain
runners.spotlight: Ready win in a 6f maiden at Hamilton (good ground) last month; 9-2 third favourite for the Woodcote at Epsom (6f, good to soft) at Epsom 12 days ago but he dropped right away in the straight; now tried in blinkers; stable has more obvious claims with Sale Shark.
runners.comment: Won on debut but disappointing at Epsom 12 days ago; now tried in blinkers

runners.name: Freedom Flame
runners.spotlight: Confirmed the promise of her Wolverhampton debut (6f, AW) when winning a nine-runner fillies' maiden at Redcar (6f, good to firm) three weeks ago; in deeper waters this time and significant progress is required; now hooded.
runners.comment: Won a Redcar fillies' maiden last month (6f, good to firm); now hooded; big step up needed

runners.name: On The Queue Tee
runners.spotlight: Unfancied for her two runs in 5f maiden/novice events last month; hinted at ability on both occasions but she needs a transformation for the extra furlong if she's to trouble today's principals.
runners.comment: Needs a transformation for the extra furlong if she's to trouble today's principals

runners.name: Alpe d'Huez
runners.spotlight: Odds-on for his Lingfield debut (6f, AW) in April and made all for a convincing success; better form when second in the Woodcote at Epsom (6f, good to soft) 12 days ago, losing his position 2f out but rallying in the final furlong, albeit no match for the impressive winner; remains capable of better and today's stiffer test at the trip should suit.
runners.comment: Rallied for 2nd in Woodcote latest, hitting a flat spot 2f out; further progress essential

runners.name: Alfred Wallace
runners.spotlight: Midfield finish in useful C&D novice on debut; much better when winning a 7f novice (good ground) at Thirsk 17 days ago, looking green and wandering around in front but always holding the runner-up (pair clear); this is a stiffer test of his credentials but he's bred to be smart (by Dubawi out of a Group 1 winner) and could well take another good step forward.
runners.comment: Good performance to win a 7f Thirsk novice last month; likely a fair bit more to come

runners.name: Dance A Jig
runners.spotlight: Chased home A Bear Affair in the Brocklesby at Doncaster when shaping with serious promise; had that rival nearly 1l behind him when second of three at Ascot in May (5f, good to firm; 3lb worse off today) and the winner was second in the Coventry Stakes here on Tuesday; should be winning races soon but he will need a good step forward for the sixth furlong if this is to be one of them.
runners.comment: Two promising 5f runs and his latest second was franked in Tuesday's Coventry Stakes

runners.name: Victory Gold
runners.spotlight: Strong in the market at 5-2 when runner-up on debut (C&D, good ground) last month, having made the running; failed to build on it when fourth of eight at Leicester three weeks ago (6f, good to firm; 8-13 favourite); the debut promise hasn't evaporated but the first-time hood needs to give him a lift.
runners.comment: Promising debut over C&D but disappointing when odds-on at Leicester since; now hooded

runners.name: Boleto
runners.spotlight: Smart pedigree and he overcame evident inexperience (off the bridle a long way out and drifted across the track in the home straight) to win a newcomers' race at Pontefract a fortnight ago (6f, good); well regarded and he is open to significant improvement.
runners.comment: Overcame inexperience to make winning debut at Pontefract (6f, good); plenty more to come

runners.name: Harlequin Sky
runners.spotlight: Shaped well amid inexperience on her Yarmouth debut (5f, good) last month; upped to 7f (Tapeta) for her second run, in which she looked in trouble before badly hampered in the final furlong; not an obvious winner at this level.
runners.comment: Debut (5f) was promising; looked beaten when badly hampered latest; not easily recommended

runners.name: Agitator
runners.spotlight: Never in the hunt at Newmarket on debut (6f, good); his sights were lowered at Windsor eight days later (6f, good to firm) and he ran on well to record a comfortable success, albeit still looking green under pressure; that form is short of the required standard and while he is open to further progress, it may come over 7f+.
runners.comment: Good step forward with Windsor win last month (6f, good to firm); in much deeper today

runners.name: King Of Cloughan
runners.spotlight: Fourth of eight on Navan debut (6f, good to yielding) in April and showed better form when second of seven at Leopardstown (7f, good) last month; shouldn't be long in shedding his maiden tag but this race is warm and Dylan Browne McMonagle rides One Number today.
runners.comment: Two promising efforts in Ireland but his trainer may hold stronger claims with One Number

runners.name: Troublesome Guest
runners.spotlight: Shaped nicely when third of seven on her Newmarket debut (6f, good) last month and highly likely to come on for that initial experience; on the downside, the fourth and fifth in that race have done little to advertise it since.
runners.comment: Promising third on Newmarket debut but the fourth and fifth have been well beaten since

runners.name: One Number
runners.spotlight: $140,000 breeze-up 2yo who is bred for dirt; there was a lot to like about his Curragh debut (6f, good to yielding) two weeks ago, looking badly in need of the experience but recording the fastest sectional for each of the final three furlongs, eventually beaten 2l by the reopposing Celeron; forecast faster ground could suit and he is a potential big improver; respected.
runners.comment: Green on Curragh debut but rattled home for second behind Celeron; likely big improver

runners.name: Our Boy Bailey
runners.spotlight: Raced exuberantly on his Roscommon debut last month (7.5f, good) and finished second of the ten runners despite conceding 9lb to the winner and third; that race has yet to throw up a winner, though, and he's likely vulnerable dropped in trip and upped in class.
runners.comment: Shaped well when second at Roscommon on debut (7.5f, good) but that form isn't working out

runners.name: Ruler's Control
runners.spotlight: Made a winning debut at the Curragh (5f, heavy) in March, ultimately scrambling home having looked set to win comfortably 150 yards from home; has changed ownership and left Jack Foley since; one of three runners for Joseph O'Brien.
runners.comment: Won a 5f maiden at the Curragh (heavy) in March; changed hands since; new yard runs three

races.race_name: CopyBet Proudly Backs UK Horse Racing Novices' Limited Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Off The Jury
runners.spotlight: In the frame on four of his five hurdling starts since switched to current stable in the autumn and beat two rivals to score on recent chasing debut at Cartmel (2m5f, good); nudged up only 1lb for that and has another big role to play here.
runners.comment: Largely consistent over hurdles for this stable and recently won a small-field chase.

runners.name: Inca De Lafayette
runners.spotlight: Delivered on earlier chasing promise when winning four-runner Plumpton contest (2m4f, good) by 22l in April; no match for progressive rival when second of four at Newton Abbot (2m5f, good) last month but was badly hampered by a mid-race faller there and might be allowed an uncontested lead here.
runners.comment: Wide-margin winner in small field in April; second to progressive rival on latest outing

runners.name: Kingston Narcissus
runners.spotlight: Bounced back to form and kept on stoutly to win by 17l on chasing debut at Hexham (2m4f, good) last month, after which he was bought out of Rebecca Menzies' stable for £25,000; makes stable debut from 8lb higher mark but it will be interesting to see what Dan Skelton can do with him.
runners.comment: Bought for £25,000 after winning by 17l on last month's chasing debut at Hexham

races.race_name: CopyBet Pay First Past The Post Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Independent Jimmy
runners.spotlight: Runner-up here (2m4f, good) on chasing debut in September and also placed at Market Rasen (2m3f, good) the following month; dropped out tamely after making a lot of the running at Uttoxeter (2m5f, good) last month, after a break, but may have needed that run and will presumably be ridden positively again over this shorter trip; interesting.
runners.comment: Pulled up last month, after a break, but his autumn form gives him a good shout

runners.name: Cawthorne Cracker
runners.spotlight: Back from break with three respectable runs this spring, perhaps most notably when third of five at Market Rasen (2m1f, good); now 0-7 over fences, though, and probably needs today's headgear switch (blinkers instead of cheekpieces) to give him a boost.
runners.comment: Back from break with three respectable runs this spring; headgear switched here

runners.name: Prairie Queen
runners.spotlight: 0-16 over hurdles but came good over fences at third attempt, making virtually all at good pace at Hereford (2m, good) six weeks ago, despite jumping slightly left throughout; 4lb higher here but this switch to an anti-clockwise track looks the right move.
runners.comment: Made virtually all at good pace at Hereford six weeks ago; a possible off 4lb higher here

runners.name: Definite Dream
runners.spotlight: 0-9 in points and 0-9 over hurdles but returned from 314-day break with very close second to progressive subsequent winner on last month's chasing debut at Stratford (2m1f, good to firm); obvious contender if in similar form here.
runners.comment: Close second to progressive subsequent winner on last month's chase debut at Stratford

runners.name: Daany
runners.spotlight: Four-time hurdle winner; runner-up on two of his three chasing attempts this spring but was no match for the winner at Fontwell (2m2f, good) last time and others in this line-up have more compelling form.
runners.comment: Second in two of his three chases this spring but others have more compelling form
runners.owner: Duckhaven D Kenealy W Sprigg M Sprigg

runners.name: Southey
runners.spotlight: Better of two chase runs for Stuart Crawford in the winter when second of four at Ayr (2m, soft); also suited by good ground but was tailed off when back over hurdles for new stable six weeks ago (had wind op soon afterwards); needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Second in small-field chase in January but tailed off over hurdles for new yard last month

runners.name: Auntie Maggie
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdle winner last spring; 0-3 over fences but posted sound effort when second of five behind much-improved rival over C&D (good) three weeks ago; likely contender again.
runners.comment: 0-3 over fences but posted sound effort when second to big improver here last month

runners.name: Caterpillar Girl
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner; still unexposed after only six runs but fell on chase/handicap debut in April and was about 27l behind Auntie Maggie when fourth of five over C&D (good) three weeks ago; needs new tongue-tie to enable improvement.
runners.comment: Unexposed after only six runs but was beaten about 33l over C&D last month

runners.name: Greer Hill
runners.spotlight: 0-6 over hurdles, having been well beaten in three handicaps this spring (2m1f-2m4f; cheekpieces added last time); last year's Irish point second offers a ray of hope on this chasing debut but it still takes a big leap of faith to support him.
runners.comment: 0-6 over hurdles, having been well beaten in three handicaps this spring; chase debut

races.race_name: CopyBet Daily World Cup Profit Boost Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Qual') (GBB) (Div I)

runners.name: Genealogy
runners.spotlight: Useful Flat racer who came second in a good-quality Ascot juvenile hurdle (1m7f, soft; also suited by good ground) for Denis Hogan in January; his two runs either side of that weren't quite as good but he still has strong claims on this stable debut.
runners.comment: Useful Flat racer who was second in a good Ascot juvenile hurdle during the winter

runners.name: Babs
runners.spotlight: Doesn't help herself by tending to race too freely but won good-ground Stratford bumper in the autumn and ran big race in defeat over hurdles at Newton Abbot (2m1f, good; winner followed up there on Friday) three weeks ago; solid place claims.
runners.comment: Ran big race in defeat at Newton Abbot last month and has solid place claims here

runners.name: Sword Of Wessex
runners.spotlight: Fairly useful on the Flat at this best; no match for ready winner when second on last month's hurdle debut at Huntingdon (2m, good to firm) but is open to improvement over jumps and can collect another minor prize.
runners.comment: Quite useful at best on the Flat; no match for winner when second on hurdle debut

runners.name: Baileys Big Image
runners.spotlight: Beaten out of sight when third of five at huge odds on recent hurdling debut at Fakenham (2m, good).
runners.comment: Beaten out of sight when third of five at huge odds on recent hurdling debut at Fakenham

runners.name: Mystic Wind
runners.spotlight: French-bred 4yo; represents a top stable but raced too freely and showed only minor promise when well-held fifth over C&D (good; hooded, as today) on debut on Thursday; may still be a work in progress.
runners.comment: In good hands but raced too freely and showed only minor promise on recent debut

runners.name: Diamond Grace
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to Scottish National winner Macdermott; beaten about 37l when fifth of eight in soft-ground Warwick bumper in February but may fare better over hurdles on faster ground.
runners.comment: Beaten about 37l in soft-ground bumper in February but may fare better over hurdles

runners.name: Palma Springs
runners.spotlight: Not beaten that far when fourth in fillies' hurdle in December but was about 25l behind Sword Of Wessex when third of six at Huntingdon (2m, good) last month and needs significant improvement here.
runners.comment: Has shown some promise but was about 25l behind Sword Of Wessex on latest outing

runners.name: Trackman
runners.spotlight: Lowly rated Flat maiden who was a tailed-off outsider on last month's hurdling debut at Huntingdon (2m, good).
runners.comment: Lowly rated Flat maiden who was a tailed-off outsider on last month's hurdling debut

runners.name: Delta Legend
runners.spotlight: Regressed on the Flat last year and was well beaten at triple-digit odds on both hurdling starts for new stable last month.
runners.comment: Well beaten at triple-digit odds on both hurdling starts last month

runners.name: Arcon
runners.spotlight: Won on the Flat last summer (1m6f; RPR 80) but was well held when third of six on hurdling debut at Huntingdon (2m, good) last spring and has fitness to prove after 258-day absence; wears first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Won on the Flat last summer but was well held when third on last spring's hurdle debut

races.race_name: CopyBet Daily World Cup Profit Boost Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Qual') (GBB) (Div II)

runners.name: Maskarvel
runners.spotlight: Best form when second at Aintree (2m1f, good) in May; only third as odds-on favourite at Stratford 18 days ago, before chasing home a red-hot Skelton favourite back over that extended 2m2f at Stratford yesterday (beaten 16l); peak form is the best in this field.
runners.comment: Peak form is best in this field; two defeats since, 16l behind red-hot favourite yesterday

runners.name: Delgany Second Now
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to high-class jumper Any Second Now and useful jumper Roxboro Road; made low-key debut when fifth of seven in Plumpton bumper (good to soft) in March but is with a good stable and ought to be capable of much better; market helpful.
runners.comment: Made low-key bumper debut in March but respresents good yard and has good pedigree

runners.name: Imperial Trooper
runners.spotlight: Just 1-18 on the Flat but showed fair form when placed twice over 1m2f this spring; can be competitive at this level if he takes well to hurdling at the first attempt.
runners.comment: Just 1-18 on the Flat but capable of fair form over middle distances; makes hurdle debut

runners.name: Provision
runners.spotlight: Placed on the Flat in April (1m6f, AW; RPR 71) and kept on for respectable third behind two subsequent winners when back over hurdles at Southwell (2m, good) last month; has a bit to find here but considered each-way.
runners.comment: Kept on for respectable third at Southwell last month and has each-way claims again

runners.name: Rogue Impact
runners.spotlight: Useful on the Flat at his best; has regressed and become inconsistent in this sphere but seemed to respond well to first-time blinkers when third at Brighton (1m4f, good to firm; RPR 79) last month; sports refitted cheekpieces for this hurdling debut; can feature if all goes to plan.
runners.comment: Inconsistent on the Flat but ran quite well in blinkers last month (RPR 79); hurdle debut

runners.name: Galeforcechopper
runners.spotlight: Probably didn't achieve a great deal when third of five in Newton Abbot bumper for David Jefferies last summer, and finished down the field in a stronger bumper in the autumn; hooded for stable/hurdle debut.
runners.comment: Didn't show enough in two bumpers for another stable last year

runners.name: Winters Hill
runners.spotlight: Finished last in two bumpers for Chris Down last year and can only be watched on this stable/hurdle debut; hood removed after one use.
runners.comment: Finished last in two bumpers for Chris Down last year; makes stable/hurdle debut

runners.name: Division Day
runners.spotlight: Runner-up twice over hurdles at start of this year (2m/2m4f) and returned from break with Flat win this month (1m4f; RPR 69); seems versatile regarding ground and ought to be a tough nut to crack.
runners.comment: Second twice over hurdles at start of this year and recently won on the Flat; big player

runners.name: Media Mogul
runners.spotlight: Fair Flat racer who made quite promising stable/hurdle debut when fourth at Huntingdon (2m, good) in November; dropped out of contention tamely at Southwell (2m, good to soft) later that month but was given a wind op afterwards and is back on better ground here; could have a say.
runners.comment: Fair Flat racer; made quite promising start to hurdle career in November; back from break

races.race_name: James Brewer 21 Years Undefeated Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Our Guide
runners.spotlight: Joined Jamie Snowden after winning Irish bumper in October and is 2-3 over hurdles this spring, the latter win gained at Stratford (2m6f, good to firm); tackles much stronger opposition on this handicap debut and ideally needs to brush up his jumping but it's hard to gauge the limit of his ability.
runners.comment: 2-3 over hurdles; tackles stronger opposition on handicap debut but remains unexposed

runners.name: Minella Rescue
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on all four starts this year, the latest over 2m7f at Uttoxeter last month (race he won in 2025); dips into a much less competitive event here, though, and the booking of Sean Bowen catches the eye.
runners.comment: Out of form this year but dips into a much less competitive race here and Sean Bowen rides

runners.name: Paddy De Pole
runners.spotlight: 4-14 over hurdles, the latest win gained at Sedgefield (3m2f, good) in September; absent since a lesser effort over 2m7f in October, though, and might not be ideally suited by this drop back in trip.
runners.comment: 3m3f winner in September; off since lesser effort over 2m7f in October

runners.name: Sure Touch
runners.spotlight: Useful chaser who signed off in the autumn with win off today's mark at Market Rasen (2m5f, good); has gone well when fresh before but is quite tricky to weigh up on this first start over hurdles since 2022.
runners.comment: Won off this mark over fences in October; not seen since; first hurdle run since 2022

races.race_name: CopyBet Bog Now Royal Ascot Tomorrow Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Roysse
runners.spotlight: Chasing didn't go to plan last season (four attempts) and he was easily reeled in after making the running on return to hurdling in March but a return to very patient tactics paid dividends at Bangor (2m, good) last month, where he cut through the field to cause a 33-1 surprise; his tendency to race too freely remains a concern but a recent 7lb rise ought to be manageable.
runners.comment: Free-goer; came from off the pace to cause 33-1 surprise at Bangor last month

runners.name: Scintillante
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner on good ground in early part of last season; below best on all three starts since returning from a break this spring, with his C&D run three weeks ago particularly disappointing, but he's well handicapped if a recent wind op proves successful.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner in 2025; needs to better recent efforts but has been given a wind op

runners.name: Ez Tiger
runners.spotlight: Made all in this race a year ago, after a seven-month break, and showed career-best form when scoring at Plumpton (2m, good to soft) in the autumn; returns from latest break on a tough mark but no surprise if he gives this a good shot.
runners.comment: Returns from eight-month break on fairly tough mark but won this race when fresh in 2025

runners.name: Arnie Moon
