runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 6yo who displayed major improvement on handicap hurdle debut at Hereford (2m, good) in March, drawing clear to win by 15l; absent since early fall at Exeter ten weeks ago but may still have more to offer.
runners.comment: Won by 15l on handicap debut in March; fell next time but may still have more to offer

runners.name: Cinammon Coco
runners.spotlight: Flat/hurdle winner in Ireland for Joseph O'Brien last year but pulled up in two hurdles for new stable towards end of last season and also struggled when back on the Flat last month; cheekpieces (worn once on the Flat) are given another whirl here.
runners.comment: Pulled up on both hurdling starts for this stable and also struggled on the Flat last time

runners.name: Bertie B
runners.spotlight: Won twice over hurdles in 2024 (2m5f/2m4f) but seemed badly to lose his way in spring 2025 and returned from 411-day absence with another poor run this month; drops back in trip with a lot to prove.
runners.comment: Out of form in spring 2025 and returned with another poor run this month; lots to prove

races.race_name: CopyBet Bog Now Royal Ascot Friday Handicap Hurdle (Div I)

runners.name: Aquifolia
runners.spotlight: Just a respectable fourth of six over C&D (good) last month, her second run after a break, and now 0-9 over hurdles but has dropped another 2lb since and enters each-way calculations.
runners.comment: Just a respectable fourth of six here last month but still enters each-way calculations

runners.name: Latin
runners.spotlight: Six-time Flat winner; well beaten on hurdling debut in Ireland in 2024 but displayed clear promise when four twice in novice company in April (2m, good), and his opening handicap hurdle mark does not look daunting.
runners.comment: Six-time Flat winner; has shown clear promise over hurdles; opening mark not daunting

runners.name: Adaay Dancing
runners.spotlight: 0-8 over hurdles with very mixed record; ran well to a point when fourth at Huntingdon (2m, good to firm; refitted hood) last month but her finishing effort was disappointing; not the percentage call here.
runners.comment: 0-8 over hurdles with very mixed record; not the percentage call

runners.name: Ufouria
runners.spotlight: 0-9 over hurdles, having failed to deliver on promise shown in Ireland last year; took good step back in right direction when third of seven at Ludlow (2m, good) last month but others here have more appealing profiles.
runners.comment: Latest run was a good step back in the right direction but others appeal more all the same

runners.name: Silent Revolution
runners.spotlight: Out of sorts for Alison Thorpe last year and well beaten on both starts for new stable this spring, the latter in a selling handicap;  sports first-time hood here; could be considered only if lively in the betting.
runners.comment: Out of sorts last year and well beaten on both starts for new stable this spring

runners.name: Tara Iti
runners.spotlight: Eight-time winner over hurdles (mostly on good ground) but out of form for current stable since the autumn; needs to turn a corner.
runners.comment: Eight-time winner over hurdles but out of form for current stable since the autumn

runners.name: Crown Of India
runners.spotlight: Left low-key reappearance run behind him when finishing well to score over C&D (good) three weeks ago and is now 2-11 over hurdles; still on workable mark after recent 4lb rise; high on the list.
runners.comment: Finished well for C&D win last month and remains on workable mark after 4lb rise

runners.name: Genbu
runners.spotlight: Held on well to make all at Uttoxeter (2m, good) last month, his third run for Sarah-Jayne Davies, and again ran well from the front when third there a fortnight ago; respected in current mood.
runners.comment: Made all at Uttoxeter last month and again ran well there three weeks ago; respected

runners.name: Bamber
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 6yo who showed a tendency to race too freely but also some significant ability when midfield in three novice/maiden hurdles last season (2m, good-soft); makes handicap debut after 100-day break but has potential.
runners.comment: Displayed ability when midfield in three qualifying races last term; handicap debut today

runners.name: Leading The Way
runners.spotlight: 0-19 under all codes and makes no significant appeal from 11lb out of the handicap; tongue-tie refitted.
runners.comment: 0-19 under all codes and makes no significant appeal from 11lb out of the handicap

races.race_name: CopyBet Bog Now Royal Ascot Friday Handicap Hurdle (Div II)

runners.name: No More Bolero
runners.spotlight: Easy winner over hurdles at Ludlow (2m, good) last November and he recorded his fourth Flat win when scoring at Bath (2m1f, good to firm) in April; wasn't beaten far back there 12 days ago and he still has potential for further progress in this sphere; interesting contender back over hurdles.
runners.comment: Hurdle win in November and he's scored on the Flat this spring; respected back over jumps

runners.name: Cawthorne Banker
runners.spotlight: Won handicaps at Fontwell (2m1f, good to soft) and Uttoxeter (2m, good) last autumn but he's been back in the doldrums since; now 3lb lower than for his last success but he needs a major revival.
runners.comment: Two wins last autumn but he's been back in the doldrums since; needs major revival

runners.name: Person Of Interest
runners.spotlight: Emphatic winner of a handicap chase at Downpatrick (2m3f, good) last August but he's generally struggled since including in three runs this spring; overall record is now 1-18 and he has something to prove back over hurdles for new yard; equipment removed.
runners.comment: Overall record of 1-18 and he has plenty to prove back over hurdles for new yard

runners.name: Mi Sueno
runners.spotlight: His last win was over two years ago but he's well treated on old form and was only beaten 3l at Stratford (2m, good to firm) last month; has each-way claims if he can back that up and he won't mind what the weather does.
runners.comment: His last win was in March 2024 but he ran respectably at Plumpton latest; not ruled out

runners.name: Master Dunraven
runners.spotlight: Fairly useful Flat racer; flopped when odds-on at Wetherby in February and is now 0-8 over hurdles but suggested his turn might be near when third at Plumpton (2m, soft) on his penultimate run; cheekpieces are now added on his return and has possibilities if he can bounce back.
runners.comment: 0-8 over hurdles and he flopped when odds-on last time; cheekpieces are added on return

runners.name: Al Mootamarid
runners.spotlight: Won with tongue-tie added in a Stratford handicap (2m, good) last June but he's been well held over hurdles and on the Flat in last two starts; may have needed his reappearance run at Chester last month but his form is patchy and he's still 6lb higher than for his hurdle win; others preferred.
runners.comment: Hurdle win last summer but his form is patchy and he needs to find more to defy this mark

runners.name: Both Of Us
runners.spotlight: Progressive triple winner (1m4f-2m) on the Flat for George Boughey last year; mixed results in three hurdle runs for new yard this spring but he's was only beaten around 6l when third in a Chepstow maiden (2m, good) last time; had wind surgery since and he needs a close look now switched to handicapping after a short break.
runners.comment: Triple Flat winner and he needs a close look on this handicap hurdle debut
runners.owner: Mrs H Watson & Mrs Carol Lee

runners.name: Peace Motivator
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner for Olly Murphy in 2024 but has failed to complete in all six hurdle starts, including when pulled up in two handicaps for current yard; still has low mileage but he's hard to recommend.
runners.comment: Bumper winner but he's failed to complete in all six hurdle runs

races.race_name: racingtv.com Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Royal Blaze
runners.spotlight: Both wins last season were over 1m2f; hasn't worn headgear so far this season and was below-par dropped to 7f last time (good to firm; reportedly never travelling) having run creditably when fourth at Ayr after a break 15 days earlier (1m1f); interesting with cheekpieces back on
runners.comment: Stays this trip and is better than he has shown on last two runs; headgear back on; chance

runners.name: Falcon Nine
runners.spotlight: Dual 1m1f winner last August (good); improved markedly on reappearance run when neck second at Pontefract last week (1m2f, good); a possible on that form.
runners.comment: Dual 1m1f winner in 2025; best run this term when 2nd at Pontefract last week; a possible

runners.name: Port Darwin
runners.spotlight: Won a Musselburgh nursery last August (1m, good); gelded before this season, but hasn't improved significantly; good second over 1m4f at Kempton when tried in blinkers (dispensed with here) in April, but not so good since.
runners.comment: 1m nursery winner in 2025; good 2nd in first-time blinkers in April but not so good since

runners.name: Book Of Life
runners.spotlight: Yet to win in 14 runs on turf (below-par both outings last season); has been in fair form without winning in a busy AW campaign this year; change of surface not sure to suit.
runners.comment: 0-14 on turf; fair form in a busy AW campaign this year; best watched back on grass

runners.name: Far Ahead
runners.spotlight: Only win so far was in a Catterick novice in May 2025 (7f, good to firm); best run this term when second at Beverley last time (8.5f, good to firm); a possible.
runners.comment: Only win to date was in a 7f Catterick novice last May; good second last time; a possible
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: Storm On Jura
runners.spotlight: Unexposed; has shown very little so far, and has been beaten 12l or more on her two starts this year; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Unexposed; only modest form (beaten over 12l on her two starts in 2026); hard to fancy

races.race_name: Scullion Law, Law Firm For Life EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B, C & D) (Qual') (GBB Race)

runners.name: Sea Mirage
runners.spotlight: Improved on his first run when staying-on second in a Leicester maiden last time (5f, good to firm); a possible on that run.
runners.comment: Improved on debut run when a staying-on second at Leicester last time; chance on that run

runners.name: Concert Pitch
runners.spotlight: Had a stiff task last time (below-par), but fair efforts on his three previous runs, two at Beverley (5f, good/good to soft) and the other at Windsor (6f, good); may find a couple too good.
runners.comment: Fair efforts on first three starts (stiff task latest); may find a couple too good

runners.name: Cheeky Chesca
runners.spotlight: Sound efforts on last two starts, finishing second at Ripon last month (6f, good to firm) and second at Wetherby last time (5.5f, good to firm; favourite); a possible.
runners.comment: Sound efforts when second at Ripon and Wetherby on last two starts; a possible

runners.name: Dubai Champion
runners.spotlight: Improved on debut run when 1.75l third to a pair of expensively-bought newcomers at Ripon last time (5f, good); chance.
runners.comment: Improved on debut when third to two costly newcomers at Ripon last time; one with a chance
runners.owner: Future Champions Racing Syn & Ben Haslam

runners.name: Sunset Cove
runners.spotlight: Showcasing filly; 57,000gns yearling; third foal; closely related to a 1m 2yo winner; dam 7f AW winner; newcomer; worth a market check.
runners.comment: 57,000gns yearling; third foal; closely related to a 1m 2yo winner; worth market check

runners.name: Le Grand Etoile
runners.spotlight: 52,000gns yearling; has finished last on both starts (6f) so far (excuses as bit slipped through his mouth on first occasion); drop in trip might suit but plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Has finished last in two 6f novices so far (excuses first time); drop to 5f might help

races.race_name: Follow Us On X @hamiltonparkrc Handicap

runners.name: Canaria Queen
runners.spotlight: Three 5f handicap wins this year, the latest over C&D last week when comfortably outpointing six rivals; the combination of a 5lb penalty and stronger opposition may find her out.
runners.comment: Three 5f wins this year, the latest over C&D last week; penalty/higher grade to overcome

runners.name: Jordan Electrics
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed an annus mirabilis in 2024, winning seven times with five of them coming at Hamilton; drew a blank in 2025 but this year's efforts have been quite encouraging, second at Newmarket in April and a close sixth in the Victoria Cup last month; the drop into Class 4 company will help but this veteran is unlikely to get the quick ground that suits him so well; one of three runners for Jim Goldie.
runners.comment: 7 wins in 2024, 5 of them here; retains plenty of ability & now drops in class; e-w shout
runners.owner: B Jordan, Brian Jordan, S Jordan & N McConnell

runners.name: Montezuma
runners.spotlight: Often threatened better as a 3yo and he has made good progress this year, albeit he only has one win (5f, good to soft) to show for it; not beaten far into fifth in a blanket finish at Thirsk 17 days ago (5f, good); now sports a hood for the first time and that could help this free-going sort.
runners.comment: Progressing this year and may not have reached his ceiling; big player with a hood added
runners.owner: B Jordan, Brian Jordan, S Jordan & N McConnell

runners.name: Top Juggler
runners.spotlight: Didn't run a bad race here for the Quinns and he turned in another solid effort at Hamilton when a close third of 13 in a 6f handicap (good ground) on last month's reappearance; has a belated first attempt at 5f today and he should give it another good go.
runners.comment: Good course record, including close 3rd on last month's return (6f); drops to 5f; chance

runners.name: Call Margot
runners.spotlight: Lightly-raced 3yo; arrives on the back of a luckless second in the 3yo Dash at Epsom (5f, good) 12 days ago, having nowhere to go for much of the final 2f but finishing with a flourish when in the clear; up 4lb; unraced on slower than good so could do with it drying out a bit but leading claims if that is the case.
runners.comment: Unlucky in the 3yo Dash at Epsom latest; up 4lb but she's on an upward curve; big player

runners.name: Solar Aclaim
runners.spotlight: Thrown in on last season's best form and there were glimmers of a revival at Epsom on Derby Day (6f, soft); drops in class and looks likely to pop up soon but drying ground won't play to his strengths now back to 5f.
runners.comment: Handicapped to win and more promise at Epsom latest; drying ground not ideal back at 5f

runners.name: Jm Jhingree
runners.spotlight: Three wins for Jim Goldie in 2025, the latest off a 5lb higher mark in December (5f, AW); poor form in three runs for new yard and will be of more interest when dropped in class.
runners.comment: Three wins for Jim Goldie in 2025; poor form for new yard; can't recommend at present

runners.name: Tiva
runners.spotlight: Retains ability and she hasn't been beaten far in either of her last two starts; wouldn't look to have much margin for error off this mark though.
runners.comment: Not beaten far on her last two starts but little room for error off this mark

runners.name: Classy Al
runners.spotlight: Quirky customer with two C&D wins on his record and now 6lb lower than for last July's success; running himself into form this year but his stable look to hold stronger claims elsewhere.
runners.comment: Quirky but capable and he's edging down the weights; trainer has stronger claims elsewhere

races.race_name: #Cleanstay At Hampton By Hilton Handicap

runners.name: Stirrup Cup
runners.spotlight: All three wins have been over 6f on soft ground, the latest an apprentice handicap at Carlisle last week when coming from well off the pace (Pop Star fourth); unpenalised; any rain a bonus.
runners.comment: Best over 6f on soft ground; unpenalised for last week's Carlisle win; may need rain

runners.name: Iris Dancer
runners.spotlight: Won her tenth race over C&D when getting up to score by a nose here a week ago (good to soft); only 2lb higher; another good run on the cards.
runners.comment: Won her tenth race over C&D last week; just 2lb higher; should go well again

runners.name: Doon The Glen
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark on turf when winning a low-grade amateur riders' event over C&D last week; has a stiffer task with her 5lb penalty in a better race.
runners.comment: Off the mark on turf when winning low-grade event over C&D a week ago; stiffer task here

runners.name: Emperor's Son
runners.spotlight: Carlisle winner on debut in June 2023 (5f, good to firm); gained another win at Wolverhampton in January (5f) and fair run back on turf when fourth at Pontefract last time (6f); a possible, but perhaps better at shorter.
runners.comment: Both wins have been at 5f, latest on Tapeta in January; fair fourth on turf latest
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: Maldevious
runners.spotlight: Ex-Hugo Palmer; ten-race maiden; has finished fourth over 6f on three of her four runs this term (good/good to firm/AW), latest a fillies' handicap at Newcastle.
runners.comment: Ex-Hugo Palmer; 0-10; fourth on three of her four starts this term (6f); stiffish task

runners.name: Annie Edson Taylor
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in first-time blinkers last September; also successful last time when getting up to win at a head at Thirsk (6f, good); up 2lb; should go well.
runners.comment: C&D winner last September; comes into this on back of a win at Thirsk last time; contender

runners.name: Catalyse
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D on his debut when with Richard Fahey (good); hasn't won since and well beaten in three starts for Jim Goldie this time round; plenty to prove at present.
runners.comment: C&D winner on debut; hasn't won since and well below-par on his three runs this year

runners.name: Pop Star
runners.spotlight: Well-backed when winning over 5f at Carlisle two runs back; not disgraced off this 5lb higher mark when fourth to Stirrup Cup over 6f (soft) on same track last week; a possible.
runners.comment: In decent form having won (5f) and been fourth (6f) at Carlisle on last two runs; chance

races.race_name: Hamilton Park Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Bearin Up
runners.spotlight: Third in hot race at Musselburgh (1m1f, good) in April and won at Newmarket (1m, good) next time; not beaten far in fifth at Nottingham (1m2f, good) last time, despite interference, and that form is working out well; this improving 3yo can be in the mix if the forecast slow ground isn't an issue.
runners.comment: Won at Newmarket last month then suffered interference at Nottingham; could be in the mix

runners.name: Porth Eilian
runners.spotlight: Had just two rivals to beat on handicap debut over C&D (good to firm) three weeks ago but she comfortably made all and this half-brother to connections' smart performer Point Lynas could have plenty more to offer; this unexposed 3yo may well take a 6lb rise in her stride; unraced on slow ground.
runners.comment: Had just two rivals to beat over C&D on handicap debut but comfortably made all
runners.owner: Julie & David Martin, Dan Hall & Partner

runners.name: Epidavros
runners.spotlight: Beaten 17 times since she last won, here (1m1f, good to soft) in September 2024; however, she's run a lot of good races in the meantime and was third of nine at Carlisle (1m, soft) recently; might not be far away.
runners.comment: On a long losing run but good third at Carlisle recently and might not be far away

runners.name: Sound Janet
runners.spotlight: On a handy mark on her best form for Roger Varian (9.4f/1m4f); however, she made it 0-7 when tailed off at 33-1 on last month's stable and seasonal debut at Newcastle (1m2f, AW); wind op since; transformation needed.
runners.comment: On a handy mark on her best form but made it 0-7 when tailed off on stable/seasonal debut

runners.name: Geo
runners.spotlight: Returned to form to win at 12-1 at Ayr (1m, good to firm) last month; showed promise on good to soft early in career; not ruled out but she was only sixth of 11 at Thirsk (1m, good) last time and needs to better that effort.
runners.comment: Won at Ayr last month and not ruled out but only sixth of 11 at Thirsk last time

runners.name: Second Fiddle
runners.spotlight: Won at Newcastle (1m, AW) in December and she's just 1lb higher today, but she's struggled back on turf on her three starts since April and a major turnaround is necessary.
runners.comment: Won at Newcastle (1m, AW) in December but she's struggled on turf this season

races.race_name: Scottish Racing Supporting #Racingwithpride Handicap

runners.name: Spiritoftheblues
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f Ayr novice on debut last August (good); rather disappointing in similar company on last two starts; needs to do better back up in trip on her handicap debut.
runners.comment: 7f novice win (debut); below that form since; could fare better up in trip on h'cap debut

runners.name: Just A Gambler
runners.spotlight: Best form was on the AW in March (first two runs after being gelded) when winning at Chelmsford and finishing third in a Kempton handicap (both at 1m); has been disappointing back on turf including with cheekpieces and blinkers; no headgear here; plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Best form was on AW on first 2 runs after being gelded, but has been disappointing since

runners.name: Attention Seeker
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden; has shaped better on last two starts, finishing fourth at Beverley (1m2f, good to firm) and Carlisle (1m1f, good to soft; pulled hard); might be suited by dropping back in trip; could go well.
runners.comment: 0-10; fourth last 2 starts, notably at Carlisle last week (1m1f, pulled hard);  a possible

runners.name: Beyond The Bar
runners.spotlight: Three sound efforts on first three starts last term, getting off the mark in a Redcar novice (1m, good) in October; gelded after a poor run the following month and yet to recapture his best form this season.
runners.comment: Won 1m Redcar novice last October; gelded after next run; not hit top form this term

runners.name: Whernside
runners.spotlight: Unexposed; three decent efforts over 7f in novice/maiden company on turf/AW; back up in trip for handicap debut (well beaten on debut previous try at 1m) and a player.
runners.comment: Unexposed; decent efforts on last three starts; back up in trip for h'cap debut; chance
runners.owner: Unique Retreats Stays Ltd And Partner

runners.name: Tarmonbarry Kid
runners.spotlight: Off the mark on second handicap start when landing moderately-run race at Doncaster last time (1m, good to soft; form fair); stiffer task off this 6lb higher mark but may well progress further.
runners.comment: Won at Doncaster on second h'cap start last time; that form fair; up 6lb; should go well

races.race_name: Hampton By Hilton Hamilton Park Handicap

runners.name: Regulus Black
runners.spotlight: Warm favourite over 1m2f (good to firm) at Beverley and Chepstow in his two handicaps and he won the latter in eventually clearcut fashion; it looks as if 1m3f will be within reach and, after just five races, there's a good chance he can find a bit more improvement; major shout despite 5lb rise.
runners.comment: Five races; 1m2f winner in his second handicap; it looks as if 1m3f will be within reach

runners.name: Uncle Liam
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden prior to his step up to 1m4f at this track (good) 13 days ago which he won by a short head, leading 5f out and fighting off a strong challenge; 3lb rise does not look harsh and he has not had much exposure over this sort of trip; could go well again.
runners.comment: 12th attempt when off the mark by a short head in battling fashion here (1m4f) last time

runners.name: Annandale
runners.spotlight: 8yo who has won eight races overall and is 5-37 on turf, with his latest win last August off 7lb higher than today; recent fourth here was a respectable return to turf, after 107 days off, so he is one to consider.
runners.comment: Latest win last August off 7lb higher; made respectable return to turf after 107 days off

runners.name: Stepanov
runners.spotlight: Five races, best efforts when twice runner-up; that includes seven-runner handicap at Wetherby (1m2f, good to firm) on latest start, when he tried front-running, and such a lightly raced sort may still be capable of better.
runners.comment: Five races; best efforts when twice runner-up, making most in a 1m2f handicap last time

runners.name: Roger Henry
runners.spotlight: Fair form twice on softer than good, from his only three turf races; absent 570 days before this season; beaten about 3l at 40-1 when sixth of 11 at Newcastle (1m4f, AW) 15 days ago, which was the first sign of significant ability remaining.
runners.comment: 570 days off before this term; latest race was first sign of significant ability remaining

runners.name: Tee Aitch Aye
runners.spotlight: Won over 1m1f here (soft) last September when 3lb higher but he's beaten only one rival in the course of his three races this year; probably stays 1m2f but well beaten in his three attempts at 1m4f; risky.
runners.comment: Has beaten only one rival in the course of his three races this year; probably stays 1m2f

runners.name: Galahad Threepwood
runners.spotlight: Dual AW Flat winner (1m4f) in 2021 who also won a 2m3f hurdle race in March 2023; that's from 44 starts and he may prove best watched on this first appearance since May 2025, despite the reduced mark.
runners.comment: May prove best watched on this first appearance since May 2025, despite the reduced mark

races.race_name: Book Online At ripon-races.co.uk Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Beauty Box
runners.spotlight: 95,000gns yearling; Space Blues half-sister to 6f 2yo winner Peel Park (RPR 81); made a promising start when fourth at Newmarket (5f, good) in April and she built on that when runner-up at Windsor (6f, good to firm) last month; sets a fair standard on that form and she could continue on an upward curve; key player.
runners.comment: Sets a fair standard on her second at Windsor and she's open to more progress; key player

runners.name: Penny Arcade
runners.spotlight: Promising second in a Thirsk maiden (5f, good) last month but she was turned over at 1-4 in a claimer at Beverley three weeks ago; left Kevin Ryan for 20,000gns since; has bit to prove after that last run but her penultimate form puts her firmly in the picture and she looks ready for this new trip.
runners.comment: Held at 1-4 in a claimer latest but her best form puts her firmly in the picture; new yard

runners.name: Chiwara
runners.spotlight: January foal; first foal; dam 6f winner (RPR 82), closely related to useful 1m 2yo winner Wise Prince, out of twice-raced sister to 7f Group 3 winner Main Aim and half-sister to 9.7f Group 2 winner Weightless; plenty to like on paper and she's an interesting newcomer.
runners.comment: Plenty to like on paper and she needs a close look on debut

runners.name: Bint Minzaal
runners.spotlight: Cost 155,000euros as a yearling but she made a low-key start when sixth of nine at Haydock (6f, good to soft; 9-1) last month; should know more this time but she needs to leave that initial form a long way behind.
runners.comment: Cost 155,000euros as a yearling but she made a low-key start when sixth of nine at Haydock

runners.name: Halcon
runners.spotlight: Tenth foal; sister to 6f-1m winner Prairie Falcon (including 2yo/Spain; RPR 82), half-sister to winners Perregrin (1m AW 2yo; 61), Ocean Wilde (5f; 52) and Falcon's Vision (Spanish 1m); dam 7f 2yo winner (78), half-sister to Group-placed 6f/1m winner Sharnberry; has fair target to aim at on debut and market should guide.
runners.comment: Sister to a fairly useful 6f-1m winner; market should be informative on debut

runners.name: Roman Crystal
runners.spotlight: 18,000euros yearling; third foal; half-sister to 6f AW winner Farandaway (RPR 84); dam 6f AW winner (73), half-sister to winners Zabaleta (5f-6f including 2yo), Bobby Boy (1m) and Relentless Harry (5f 2yo/7f); yard 0-12 with 2yos this season and she could be one for later on.
runners.comment: 18,000euros yearling; yard 0-12 with 2yos this season and she could be one for later on

runners.name: Travellers Girl
runners.spotlight: 1,500gns breeze-up 2yo; half-sister to winners Patsy Fagan (6f; RPR 83), Elzaam Tales (7f 2yo; 78), Global Wonder (1m-2m Flat; 74; 2m hurdle) and Gap Year (Italian 1m); dam 1m2f turf/1m4f AW winner (83), family of Arc winner All Along; has quite a bit of stamina in her pedigree and she could be a longer-term prospect.
runners.comment: Has quite a bit of stamina in her pedigree and she could be a longer-term prospect

races.race_name: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Leeds Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Kilfrush Desert
runners.spotlight: Promising third on Beverley debut (5f, good) last spring and she built on that when runner-up on her 6f comeback at Newcastle (reported to have bled from the nose) in April; just about sets the standard on that latest form and she's respected back on turf.
runners.comment: Runner-up on comeback at Newcastle (6f) in April and has leading claims on that form

runners.name: Grasmere Boy
runners.spotlight: Gelding by Harry Angel; nearly caused a surprise at 33-1 on Newcastle debut (7f, AW) last month and he went close again in a clear second at Catterick (7f, good to firm) last time; should be fine on this drop in trip and another bold bid is likely.
runners.comment: Has gone close in both runs this spring and he's strongly respected on this drop in trip

runners.name: Masai Angel
runners.spotlight: Tailed off on his debut at Hamilton (6f, good to soft) last June but he returned after a gelding operation with an encouraging fourth at Newcastle (6f, AW) in April; should benefit from that comeback run and has possibilities if he can take another step forward.
runners.comment: Some promise at Newcastle in April and has claims if he can build on that

runners.name: Likeadyinwasp
runners.spotlight: Finished down the field at big prices in three AW runs (5f/6f) in April; has plenty to find on turf debut and minor handicaps will be more suitable.
runners.comment: Down the field in three AW runs in April and minor handicaps will be more suitable

runners.name: Kilfrush Blue
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in sole run for Jack Channon last summer and was ninth of ten at Doncaster (6f, good; 50-1) on his stable debut last month.
runners.comment: Has struggled in two runs for two different yards and latest was at Doncaster last month
runners.owner: G P Racing And Mrs R Carr

races.race_name: Lloyd Land Rover Ripon Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Winter Flower
runners.spotlight: Won small-field Newcastle novice (1m2f, AW) last autumn but has mixed record in handicaps since; looks interesting on her second at Newcastle (12.3f) in November but she reappeared with a disappointing run at Haydock (10.4f, good to firm) in April; still lightly raced but she needs to step up again after that last run.
runners.comment: Has mixed record in handicaps but she looks interesting on her best form

runners.name: Scottish Dancer
runners.spotlight: Won on stable debut at Thirsk (1m6f) last month and he went close over 2m here two weeks later; held in 9l third when returned to hurdling (2m4f) last time but that was a fair effort and he's respected back in this sphere; has set the pace in his last two Flat runs and he's versatile ground-wise.
runners.comment: No joy back over hurdles latest but he won on the Flat last month; respected

runners.name: Something
runners.spotlight: First two wins were at Newcastle (12.4f, AW) and last two have been over C&D (good to soft/good); his last success was on his stable debut last month and he backed that up with a front-running second at Pontefract (1m4f) last week; that was a four-runner race but he chased home a progressive stablemate who has scored again since; respected off same mark.
runners.comment: Won over C&D on stable debut and was a close second at Pontefract last week; respected

runners.name: Dream Harder
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on his Chester win (1m4f, good) last May but he's 0-10 since and was well held in his final five runs for Ian Williams; returns after 200 days off and he needs to get back on track for new yard.
runners.comment: Well held in final five runs last year and has something to prove on return for new yard

runners.name: Spirit Of Jura
runners.spotlight: Four wins over 1m-1m5f last January-August, the first three on the AW and the last on good ground; still looks feasibly treated but she hasn't really fired in three runs this year and needs to rediscover her spark.
runners.comment: Hasn't really fired in three runs this year and she needs to rediscover her spark

runners.name: Raulin
runners.spotlight: Three wins over 1m2f (good/good to firm) last season; only 1lb higher than for his last success but he hasn't been in peak form in four starts this term; still unexposed at this trip but he needs to raise his game.
runners.comment: Triple 1m2f winner last season but he hasn't been in peak form this term; needs more

races.race_name: Wells Memorial Challenge Trophy Handicap

runners.name: Glory Hyde
runners.spotlight: 5yo who has won three of her last four starts including a clearcut success over C&D (good) two weeks ago; up 7lb but that was a personal best last time and she seems versatile ground-wise; big player.
runners.comment: Has won three of her last four including a clearcut win over C&D latest; big player

runners.name: Mark's Choice
runners.spotlight: Ten of his 12 wins have been at Ripon including seven on fast and slow ground over C&D; hasn't threatened in three runs this season but he was a respectable second at Catterick (6f) last time and he's in the mix back at this track.
runners.comment: Ten of his 12 wins have been at Ripon and he's in the mix back at this track

runners.name: Beattie Is Back
runners.spotlight: Triple AW winner but he's 1-13 on turf and was beaten 10l behind Glory Hyde over C&D two weeks ago; has something to prove and all of his wins have been over 7f.
runners.comment: Record of 1-13 on turf and he was beaten 10l by Glory Hyde over C&D two weeks ago
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: Bye Law
runners.spotlight: Won Ayr maiden and Redcar novice last summer; 0-7 in handicaps but he's on a reduced mark and was a creditable third of 13 at Thirsk (6f, good) two weeks ago; that was his best run for some time and he has claims if he can build on it.
runners.comment: Creditable third off reduced mark at Thirsk and has claims if he can build on that

runners.name: Beyond Borders
runners.spotlight: Triple turf winner who made it 3-5 on AW when leading close home at Wolverhampton (6f; tongue-tie added) two weeks ago; up 4lb but he beat a clear second last time and he's respected back in this sphere.
runners.comment: Triple turf winner who made it 3-5 on AW when scoring at Wolverhampton latest; respected

runners.name: Blind Beggar
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on his Sandown win (5f, good) last July but has mixed record since and he's been well held in three runs this year; now drops back in grade but he needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: On dangerous mark but he's struggled in three runs this year; needs to turn things around
runners.owner: New Vision Bloodstock & Steel A Win

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Melody De Vega
runners.spotlight: Promising third on handicap debut at Kempton (1m, AW) last month but she's taken two backward steps since; mark is sliding but she was beaten 9l over this trip at Salisbury last time and others are more convincing.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 3yo but she has a mixed record and others are more convincing
runners.owner: Michael P Tudor & J P M O'Connor

runners.name: Krissy
runners.spotlight: Progressive since November, particularly on good ground this spring; gained most recent win at Carlisle (1m1f) last month and she had an excuse when she stumbled leaving the stalls at Thirsk last time (unable to adopt usual front-running tactics); still looks on a workable mark and she's in the mix.
runners.comment: Improver who has won three times this spring and she had an excuse at Thirsk last time

runners.name: Eloquencia
runners.spotlight: Sea The Stars filly who is a half-sister to seven winners including Pimento (Group-placed 1m AW: RPR 112); progressive form in her three qualifying runs this spring and her latest second was behind an 8-13 shot at Bath (1m2f, good to firm); should have more to offer now switched to handicaps and she's an interesting contender for in-form yard; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Unexposed filly who showed clear promise in novice events; interesting on handicap debut

runners.name: Glasgow Kiss
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden who has not really progressed but she made some late gains after a slow start in a Nottingham handicap (8.3f, good) two weeks ago; worth a try at this new trip and she could get involved if first-time blinkers work.
runners.comment: Has not really progressed so far but she looks worth a try at this new trip; blinkers on

runners.name: Bay Dream Believer
runners.spotlight: All of her seven turf wins have been on good/good to firm but she's effective on good to soft; well held in first three runs this season but she got back on the scoresheet with a strong finish at Pontefract (1m2f, good) two weeks ago; up 3lb for that narrow success but she's 3-6 over C&D and she defied this mark here last summer; key player.
runners.comment: Record of 3-6 over C&D and she scored at Pontefract two weeks ago; key player

runners.name: Rosemary May
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden at Ayr (1m, good) last April but she's finished ahead of only one rival in six runs in handicaps since; was beaten 32l off this mark at Carlisle last week and she has a lot to prove at this new trip.
runners.comment: Has beaten only one rival in her six handicaps and has a lot to prove

runners.name: The New Bay Pearl
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden who failed to progress for Roger Varian and has been well held in two runs for new yard (7f/1m) this spring; her blindfold was removed late here two weeks ago but she needs to raise her game at this new trip.
runners.comment: Ten-race maiden and she needs to raise her game at this new trip

runners.name: Angela's Baar
runners.spotlight: Went close on Pontefract debut (6f, good) last June but she's been disappointing since and was beaten 18l in a Carlisle handicap (1m, soft) last week; still lightly raced but she needs a transformation on this step up to 1m2f.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 3yo but she needs a transformation on this step up to 1m2f

races.race_name: We're Back For Ladies' Day Tomorrow Handicap

runners.name: You Mystify Me
runners.spotlight: Showed some promise behind smart rivals in novice events this spring but she's struggled in last two runs including on handicap debut at Catterick (5f, good to firm) last month; still early days but she needs a transformation with visor added.
runners.comment: Unexposed filly but she needs a transformation with visor added
runners.owner: N Bradley Racing 29 & South Bank Racing

runners.name: Doralee
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 20 starts was in a classified at Redcar (5f, good to firm) last August; finished down the field there in last two runs but she had some close calls in April including in a 14-runner handicap at Doncaster; has possibilities if she can recapture that form and she's versatile ground-wise.
runners.comment: Record of 1-20 but has claims on the pick of her form this season; not ruled out

runners.name: Arlington
runners.spotlight: All five wins have been on AW and latest was at Southwell (5f) in January; 0-12 on turf but he's on a lower mark in this sphere and is well handicapped on his close second over C&D (good) last summer; usually races prominently and it wouldn't be a surprise if he made bold bid after a short break.
runners.comment: All five wins have been on AW but he's on a dangerous mark and has form over C&D

runners.name: Tanaka
runners.spotlight: Failed to beat a rival in two starts for Kevin Ryan, and has not done much better since joining David O'Meara; still unexposed but she needs major improvement to get involved.
runners.comment: Poor form in all five runs, with a best RPR of 29; needs major improvement

runners.name: What A Tahoo
runners.spotlight: 0-8 but she was placed on her turf debut here last month and backed that up with a close third in another 6f handicap, at Carlisle (good); looks interesting off unchanged mark and she should get a good pace on this drop back in trip.
runners.comment: 0-8 but she's unexposed on turf and was a close third at Carlisle latest; interesting
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: Brain Freeze
runners.spotlight: Off the mark with a brave win in a Redcar handicap (5f) last month; up 3lb but that was a personal best last time and she's a big player again if she can back that up; best form has been on good to firm but she still has low mileage.
runners.comment: Won at Redcar (good to firm) latest and she's a big player again if she can back that up

runners.name: Barmyblade
runners.spotlight: Won two in a row over 6f at Ripon (good) last May but he's been out of sorts this season and was last eight back here three weeks ago; now 9lb lower than for his last success but he needs a major revival back in trip; cheekpieces reapplied.
runners.comment: Dual course winner (6f), but he's been out of sorts this season and needs a major revival

runners.name: Hyrcanian
runners.spotlight: 16-race maiden; hasn't shown much in her two runs this season and was withdrawn after getting loose before the start at Newcastle three weeks ago; opposable.
runners.comment: 16-race maiden who hasn't shown much in her two runs this season; opposable

runners.name: Dunnington Lad
runners.spotlight: His last two wins have been over C&D (soft/good) but latest was in August 2024; well handicapped on his old form but he's been out of sorts in his three runs this season and needs a big turnaround; tongue-tie removed.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner but he's struggled this season and needs a big turnaround

races.race_name: Trustmark Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Bridget's Baby
runners.spotlight: Improved from her first three starts when beaten half a length into second of nine in a 0-50 classified event at Leicester (5f, good) nine days ago; still open to further progress, but this looks tougher.
runners.comment: Narrowly beaten in a 0-50 classified event at Leicester nine days ago; this looks tougher

runners.name: Sonic Si
runners.spotlight: Made it 14th-time lucky at Nottingham (5f, good to soft) last month and followed up under a positive ride at Chepstow (5f, good to soft) on Monday, taking his turf record to 33211; respected despite his 5lb penalty.
runners.comment: Bids for a hat-trick under a 5lb penalty after winning at Chepstow on Monday; respected

runners.name: Level Up
runners.spotlight: Losing run up to 13 and he finished over 4l behind Sonic Si when fifth of seven at Chepstow (5f, good to soft) on Monday; 1-1 here but he needs to do better than that latest effort.
runners.comment: 1-1 here, but finished over 4l behind Sonic Si at Chepstow on Monday; needs to do better

runners.name: Vape
runners.spotlight: Won twice over 6f at Windsor (good to firm) and Chepstow (good) last August; may have needed last month's return at the latter track (7f, good to firm), but this trip looks on the sharp side.
runners.comment: Won twice over 6f last August and may have needed his reappearance, but further suits best

runners.name: So Smart
runners.spotlight: Losing run up to 13, but running well in defeat lately and fared best of those on his side when fourth of nine at Chepstow (5f, soft) 11 days ago; record over C&D reads 314; player.
runners.comment: Losing run up to 13, but running well in defeat lately; record over C&D reads 314; player

runners.name: Hallandale Beach
runners.spotlight: 0-5 but has finished third in both handicaps, the latest from a bad draw at Windsor on Monday (5f, good to firm); still unexposed and worth a second look.
runners.comment: 0-5 but has finished third in both handicaps, the latest at Windsor on Monday; unexposed

races.race_name: Carmarthenshire Hunt Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands C and D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Past Passion
runners.spotlight: Beaten 4l into second of 12 at Ascot (5f, good) last month, but that was an improvement from her debut and the winner is a nice filly; a couple of those behind won next time and she looks the one to beat.
runners.comment: Second of 12 behind a nice filly at Ascot last month; looks the one to beat

runners.name: Tango Hotel
runners.spotlight: Glimpse of ability in both starts, the latest when fifth of seven in a valuable Musselburgh seller (5f, good; 9-4) 11 days ago; shaped as though in need of further, but she can't be dismissed in this field; cheekpieces are given a go.
runners.comment: Glimpse of ability in both starts and can't be dismissed in this field; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Cavan Lady
runners.spotlight: Sent off 3-1 when third of five on her debut in a valuable Goodwood seller (5f, soft) ten days ago; shaped as though another furlong might suit, but this is no more demanding; player.
runners.comment: Third on her debut in a valuable Goodwood seller ten days ago; this is no more demanding

runners.name: Leading Raya
runners.spotlight: 250-1 when tailed off on her Windsor debut (6f, good to soft) nine days ago.
runners.comment: Tailed off on her Windsor debut (6f, good to soft) nine days ago

runners.name: Nasserein
runners.spotlight: Hinted at ability when fifth of nine on last month's Bath debut (5.7f, firm; 18-1); still needs to improve, though.
runners.comment: Hinted at ability when fifth on last month's Bath debut, but still needs to improve

runners.name: Defiant Dream
runners.spotlight: 80-1 when well beaten on her Lingfield debut (6f, AW) 13 days ago; cheekpieces given a go, but she still needs to leave that effort behind; stable also runs Leading Raya.
runners.comment: Well beaten on Lingfield AW debut 13 days ago; needs to leave that behind; cheekpieces on

races.race_name: Blackmore Building Contractors Handicap

runners.name: Red Moon
runners.spotlight: Improved when making a successful handicap debut in an apprentice event at Salisbury (7f, good) on Sunday; escapes a penalty for that and holds strong claims provided this doesn't come too soon.
runners.comment: Made a successful handicap debut at Salisbury on Sunday; escapes a penalty for that

runners.name: Kingofthecarnival
runners.spotlight: Made a successful reappearance/handicap debut at Newcastle (7f, AW) in March, but hasn't really kicked on since; something to prove now making his turf debut in first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Not built on successful reappearance/handicap debut; makes his turf debut; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Phantom Shadow
runners.spotlight: Ran his best race when second of five at Bath (1m, firm) last month, but finished last of three over the same C&D (firm) 13 days later; first-time tongue-tie needs to have a positive effect.
runners.comment: Runner-up at Bath last month, but last of three back here next time; tongue-tie on

runners.name: Thai Princess
runners.spotlight: Best of three starts on the AW when fourth of 11 in a Wolverhampton maiden (7f) last time, since when she has undergone wind surgery; now makes her handicap/turf debut and well worth a market check.
runners.comment: Makes her handicap/turf debut having undergone wind surgery; well worth a market check

runners.name: Senor Campanaro
runners.spotlight: Landed a four-runner Newcastle nursery (7f, AW; first start since being gelded) in December, but hasn't shown a great deal in his other six outings including four on turf; opposable despite the drop in grade.
runners.comment: Landed a four-runner Newcastle nursery in December, but not shown a great deal otherwise

runners.name: Who Is Alice
runners.spotlight: Won a Chepstow maiden (7f, good to firm) for Jack Channon last July and then twice runner-up in nurseries, but well beaten in both starts since returning for this yard last month; best watched for now.
runners.comment: Well beaten in both starts since returning for this yard last month; best watched for now

runners.name: Distillation
runners.spotlight: Finished 6l behind the winner when second of nine on handicap debut at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) 15 days ago, but that was still an improvement on previous efforts; each-way claims again.
runners.comment: Improved when runner-up on handicap debut at Wolverhampton last time; each-way claims

runners.name: Katie's Song
runners.spotlight: Out of the frame in three starts over 7f on the AW during the spring (hinted at ability on debut); open to progress now making her handicap/turf debut and market support should be heeded.
runners.comment: Out of the frame in three starts over 7f on AW, but could improve on handicap/turf debut

runners.name: Gelato
runners.spotlight: Well held in six runs in Ireland last season and no real improvement on her stable debut/reappearance at Bath (5.7f, good to firm) last month; needs to improve for the longer trip.
runners.comment: Well held in seven starts including on stable debut; needs to improve for the longer trip

races.race_name: RHN Travel Handicap

runners.name: Factual
runners.spotlight: Record in novices read 121 and he ran well when fourth of 12 in a 0-105 contest on handicap debut at Chester (7.6f, good) last month; reportedly didn't handle the undulations when disappointing badly at Carlisle (7.7f, good to firm) last time, but a major player if putting that behind him.
runners.comment: In fine form before disappointing badly last time; major player if putting that behind him

runners.name: Great Dream
runners.spotlight: Won a Newcastle maiden (7f, AW) for the Crisfords last September; having been gelded he has only managed midfield finishes in both starts since returning for this yard last month, albeit he met trouble on the first occasion; could fare better with cheekpieces given a go.
runners.comment: Midfield in both starts for this yard, but could fare better with cheekpieces on

runners.name: Sunny Smile
runners.spotlight: Showed ability as a 2yo including finishing first and second in two starts at Hamilton (6f); never figured on his Epsom reappearance (7f, good to soft; first-time cheekpieces) 12 days ago, but possible he didn't handle the track; not written off.
runners.comment: Never figured on Epsom reappearance 12 days ago, but possible he didn't handle the track

runners.name: Dagger Strike
runners.spotlight: Did well in his first four handicaps on the AW during the winter (1222), but last two efforts have been modest including on his return to turf last time; needs the switch from cheekpieces to blinkers to make a difference.
runners.comment: Last two efforts have been modest including on his return to turf last time; blinkers on

runners.name: Huscal
runners.spotlight: 0-12 in handicaps, but is edging down the weights and ran well to finish fourth of ten at Windsor (1m, good to firm) last time (winner landed a 0-105 handicap at York last Friday); worth considering with cheekpieces given a go.
runners.comment: Edging down the weights and ran well when fourth at Windsor last time; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Slight Of Foot
runners.spotlight: Well-bred gelding who looked promising when second of 14 in a Kempton novice (7f, AW) last September, but he hasn't had a clear shot at things in either outing since; could rebound with blinkers now given a go.
runners.comment: Hasn't had a clear shot at things in either outing since runner-up at Kempton; blinkers on

runners.name: Marlborough Place
runners.spotlight: Limerick maiden winner (1m, good) for Adrian Murray last summer; having been gelded he wasn't disgraced when fourth of five on stable debut at Kempton (7f, AW) in February, but gave the impression he needed a return to further.
runners.comment: Limerick maiden winner last summer; seemed to find 7f too sharp on stable debut

races.race_name: Blackmore Building Contractors Handicap

runners.name: Quick Turn
runners.spotlight: Less exposed than most of these and he was off the mark at the seventh attempt with an improved effort at Wolverhampton (8.5f, AW) in March; well held next time but his return to turf went well when short-head second at Leicester (1m, good; front pair came clear) on Saturday; backs up quickly from the same mark and he's an obvious contender if reproducing that effort; due a 4lb rise in future.
runners.comment: Narrow second (1m, good; pair clear) on Saturday; obvious claims from the same mark

runners.name: Alice's Influence
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who finished fourth to Quick Turn on AW in March then dead-heated at Wetherby (1m, good to firm) in a new visor one month later; just pegged back at Bath eight days on but she was below par at Chepstow 19 days ago; the least exposed of these and no surprise if she bounces back.
runners.comment: Dead-heated (1m, good to firm) with visor in April; below par latest but could bounce back

runners.name: Rosemary's Rose
runners.spotlight: Two 1m wins on AW this spring, in a Lingfield classified event and a Kempton handicap, and she was close to her best when second over 7f back on AW 16 days ago; limited evidence to go on if the ground softens but otherwise acts on turf and should make her presence felt.
runners.comment: Two 1m AW wins in spring; close to best latest; rain a query but should make presence felt

runners.name: Fifty Sent
runners.spotlight: Two runs here include soft-ground C&D defeat of Bobby Dassler in August; also landed a pair of 1m AW classified events at Southwell in March and wasn't disgraced returned to turf handicaps on his last two starts; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Soft-ground C&D winner; pair of AW wins in spring and respectable on turf last two starts

runners.name: Pay Attention
runners.spotlight: Completely missed the break but showed best form since last July when second at Newbury (1m, good) in this first-time headgear combination one month ago; 1lb nudge still leaves him below his winning mark but he's been a multiple non-runner when ground has turned softer than good and won't want the forecast rain to arrive.
runners.comment: Missed break when second latest; mark still fine but probably doesn't want rain to arrive

runners.name: Devious Devan
runners.spotlight: Chucked in on what he achieved as a 3yo (includes C&D second) and finally rediscovered some form when taking AW handicap (8.5f) on penultimate start; didn't back it up when seventh of 11 over C&D (good to soft) a fortnight ago, however.
runners.comment: Rediscovered some form when taking AW h'cap two starts back; didn't back it up next time

runners.name: Bobby Dassler
runners.spotlight: Placed three times from four starts here but his regular hunting ground is Bath and he scored there (1m, heavy) off 5lb lower wearing a new hood/tongue-tie combination in October; kept to that track until the hood was removed in ten-runner handicap at Chepstow (1m, good) on Monday when he saw it well to score under Mason Paetel; carries 4lb penalty but won't mind what the weather does; player.
runners.comment: Heavy-ground winner won also won on Monday (good) with hood removed; player under penalty

races.race_name: Oil 4 Wales Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Lady Magu
runners.spotlight: Encouraging first go when fifth of 12 at Lingfield (AW) in April but, like today's rival Evening Fades, she underwhelmed at Goodwood (also 1m2f, good) 25 days ago; good chance if tapping back into that initial promise.
runners.comment: Encouraging first go but she also underwhelmed 25 days ago; way too soon to write her off

runners.name: Ziata
runners.spotlight: In the frame on all three qualifying runs and it was a respectable handicap debut when fifth of 12 at Nottingham (8.5f, good to soft) four weeks ago; newly fitted blinkers may sound a note of caution on this drop back into maiden company, but holds leading form claims.
runners.comment: Newly fitted blinkers sound a note of caution but leading form claims back in a maiden

runners.name: Poor Relation
runners.spotlight: Caught the eye when fifth on debut at Lingfield (form worked out well) in December and built on that when runner-up at Southwell (also 1m, AW) the following month; went for home a long way out before finishing well-beaten third on her comeback/turf debut at Salisbury (1m2f, soft) last Tuesday; contender to build on that if this doesn't come too soon.
runners.comment: Went for home a long way out on turf debut last week; player if this doesn't come too soon

runners.name: Evening Fades
runners.spotlight: Did best of the debutantes when fairly close fourth at Lingfield (1m, AW) in December, but it was an underwhelming return when beaten just over 11l at Goodwood (1m2f, good) last month; bred to be better than that and no surprise if she takes a step forward.
runners.comment: Good debut but it was an underwhelming return last month; bred to be better than that

runners.name: Feelin' Free
runners.spotlight: By Nathaniel; eighth foal; half-sister to 1m4f AW (RPR 68) and 2m/2m1f jumps (in Italy) winner Sea Battle; dam US 8.5f/9.5f turf winner, half-sister to US 9.5f Grade 2 winner Willcox Inn; one of two unraced fillies for Jack Jones and she's probably one for further down the line unless the market speaks positively.
runners.comment: One of two unraced fillies for Jack Jones; probably one for further down the line

runners.name: Alikalu
runners.spotlight: 2,000gns foal by Ulysses; second foal; dam 1m/1m2f winner (including 2yo; RPR 99), half-sister to useful winners Third Dimension (7f-9.5f) and Heading North (7f 2yo), out of 1m winning half-sister to Listed winners Heavenly Whisper (1m) and Gipsy Moth (5f); one of two newcomers for respected yard but probably best watched unless the market suggests otherwise.
runners.comment: 2,000gns foal by Ulysses; best watched unless the market suggests otherwise

runners.name: Blue Tulip
runners.spotlight: 100-1 when beaten similar margins in a pair of AW events (1m/1m2f) recently; not disgraced when fifth of 14 a fortnight ago but will need more on this turf debut.
runners.comment: Not disgraced when fifth of 14 a fortnight ago but will need more on this turf debut

runners.name: Miss Malone To You
runners.spotlight: Similar form in two starts for Michael Bell, latterly when last of five at Ripon (1m2f, good) in April; picked up for 7,000gns the following month and now sports a tongue-tie on this debut for her jump-orientated yard.
runners.comment: Last of five in April; sold for 7,000gns following month; tongue-tie on for stable debut

races.race_name: Josh Perry BPRFC Supporters Player Of The Year Handicap

runners.name: Zooter
runners.spotlight: 0-7; second in 9.4f AW maiden in April and third of five in handicap at Leicester (upped to 1m2f, good to firm) latest; tried cheekpieces on penultimate start; this could be a weak race but he may still prove vulnerable.
runners.comment: Third of five in handicap at Leicester (upped to 1m2f) latest; may still prove vulnerable

runners.name: Grand Pier
runners.spotlight: Dam 1m6f winner; tailed off at Bath (1m, good to soft) in April but her Newbury performance (1m2f, good) four weeks later was much better, staying on again for sixth of 14 after a shortage of room over 1f out; dropped 2lb and 7lb claimer is enlisted; should be a big player if he goes on the ground.
runners.comment: Much better again on latest start; should be a big player if he goes on the ground

runners.name: Blue Jammin
runners.spotlight: Seventh start as 2yo when he won a weak Chepstow nursery (1m, soft) in September off 3lb lower; behind at Windsor on both starts this term, despite good to soft ground last time; upped in trip but others are preferred.
runners.comment: 2yo win on soft; behind at Windsor both starts this term, despite good to soft last time

runners.name: Rajwaah
runners.spotlight: Well-bred filly who finished towards rear in two runs at Kempton (7f/1m, AW) in April and one at Windsor (1m, good; 150-1) in May; lacks any solid form but going handicapping (she's also upped in trip) surely offers a much more realistic chance and she's one to keep an eye on.
runners.comment: No solid form; going handicapping (also upped in trip) offers a much more realistic chance

runners.name: Liveinthelight
runners.spotlight: Fifth of eight in a steadily run maiden at Kempton (1m3f, AW) in March seemed better than her turf starts either side; has changed hands since latest start (same trainer); market may guide on this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Changed hands since latest start (same trainer); market may guide on this handicap debut
runners.owner: Lady Bamford , Mrs Collins & Bv Sangster

runners.name: Tales Old As Time
runners.spotlight: Third of ten in a newcomers race at Kempton (7f, AW) last August, but two backward steps in the autumn; latest was on heavy going, so might be excused and he needs a second look on this handicap/seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Third at Kempton (7f, AW) last August on debut; two backward steps in the autumn
